- https://www.nikkei.com/nkd/company/history/yprice/?scode=9697
Recovery caught up 9 names
In plain terms: the company had a soft year recently (revenue or profit fell), and this year it has turned and grown again — and the stock has confirmed the turnaround. Rule used: prior 1-2 years had a Q3 or Q4 tag (decline year), current year is Q1 (all-up). Why this matters: the pattern is "V-recovery validated by the market." These companies are often coming out of an investment cycle (R&D harvest, capex commissioning), a product transition (new platform launching), or post-restructuring. The market has now priced the recovery in, which means much of the easy gains may be behind. What to watch: whether the recovery sustains for a second year (= candidate for the sustained-compounder bucket next year), or fades back into mixed results. These names sit between "early-stage compounders" and "one-year bounces."
2317 Systena Corporation SI_IT_services mid
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 2317 + Systena IR 短信 2026/5/13 + 松井証券 2317 + minkabu 2317 settlement + Nikkei TDnet 2025/10/30 Q2 + kabuyoho 2317 + bitget wiki Systena + scouter 2317 + IFIS 株予報 2317 verification on SDV + AI PMO concentration + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: minkabu 2317 + kabutan 2317 + Systena IR
What happened: Systena is an independent IT-services company that grew from software testing / quality assurance roots. Today it operates three segments: 'Digital Integration business' (enterprise DX, system development, QA), 'Business Solution business' (proprietary products, ERP, business systems), and 'Next-generation Mobility business' (automotive embedded development, autonomous driving, connected cars). FY3/2026 revenue ¥94.40 billion (+12.9%), operating profit ¥15.37 billion (+27.3%), ordinary profit ¥16.15 billion (+36.2%), net profit ¥11.31 billion (+33.4%) — strong growth across all metrics. EPS rose to ¥31.65 (from ¥23.17). Why: Profit growth was driven by three pillars: 'Next-gen Mobility growth,' 'Digital Integration strength,' and 'Business Solution special demand.' Sustained expansion in auto-industry software-defined vehicle (SDV) adoption, autonomous-driving development, and connected-car software demand. Plus a one-time large 'special-demand' Business Solution project lifted earnings. Stock reaction: Systena stock rose over +50% in 2025. Structural drivers: First, extraordinarily strong profit growth rates (OP +27.3%, ordinary +36.2%, net +33.4%). Few SI companies sustain this magnitude of profit growth — institutional capital accumulated. Second, Next-gen Mobility's SDV theme. The auto industry's transition to SDVs, autonomous driving, and connected services is a decade-long structural investment theme; Systena is embedded in Toyota / Honda / Nissan supply chains. Highly valued by institutional investors as a long-term growth theme. Third, sustained dividend hike and stronger returns. Dividend ¥14 → ¥18 — higher payout ratio. Fourth, broad customer base. Customers span auto, telecom, finance, retail, manufacturing — resilient to industry-specific downturns. Fifth, response to TSE PBR-improvement request. Buybacks, cross-shareholding reductions improved institutional perception. Risks: (a) sustainability of current profit level unclear — FY27 guidance shows net profit -6.0% as special demand rolls off, (b) labor-cost inflation from IT-talent competition, (c) project declines if auto-industry EV investment slows. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥94.4 billion (+12.9%), OP ¥15.367 billion (+27.3%) — sharp growth. Next-gen Mobility + Digital Integration growth + Business Solution special demand contributed. DX investment + AI utilization expansion is tailwind. 'AI Datacenter Promotion Office' newly established for market research + tech-requirements verification — if commercialized, becomes a new area. Stock ¥385.0, PER 15.6x forecast, ROE 24.04%. 12 consecutive quarters of improvement; revenue, margins, equity ratio, EPS all uptrending.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
Prior decline turned this year — business and stock both up in 2025. Stock flat/— YoY confirmed the inflection. Fresh recovery; whether it extends is the predictive question.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (recovery — clean ◎):* FY3/2025 actual: revenue ¥83,621M (+8.7% YoY), operating profit ¥12,067M (+24.2% YoY), recurring profit ¥11,855M (+19.2%), net profit ¥8,480M (+17.3%). Annual dividend ¥12/share. Margin expansion: OP growth ~3x revenue growth. FY3/2026 guidance: revenue ¥89,100M (+6.6%), OP ¥12,700M (+5.2%). Stock ¥312 (Apr 7) – ¥555 (Oct 31) per Nikkei — strong recovery, +78% off the spring low. Stock-move catalyst: FY3/2025 record results announced June 2025 → margin expansion driven by Solution Design segment recovery from FY24 project-margin pressure. 業績 ◎ (revenue + profit acceleration) / 株価 ◎ (recovery).

Cautious side: Solution Design segment shows project-margin volatility (FY24 stumble); competitive IT services pricing pressure; multi-year stock derating 2021–2023 lacks sourced cause and may reflect broader IT-services sector rotation rather than company-specific story.

Sign summary: revenue rose +8.7% YoY; operating profit rose +24.2%; net profit rose +17.3%; record results; first turn to clean profit growth in years; shares rallied to ¥555 high (Oct 31); business strong, stock strong.


Sources:
- https://www.bridge-salon.jp/report_bridge/archives/2024/05/240529_2317.html (FY3/2024 details)
- https://www.bridge-salon.jp/report_bridge/archives/2025/06/250604_2317.html (FY3/2025 details)

3803 Image Information Inc. SI_IT_services small
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +5.2%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 3803 + minkabu 3803 + 株予報Pro 3803 + marke-predict.com 3803 analysis on 2025/9/5 limit-up + traders.co.jp 3803 + 松井証券 3803 + Marke Predict 3803 analysis + note marke_tantan article on 2025/9/5 limit-up causes including Miyama 5.1% large-shareholder report + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: Yahoo Finance 3803 + minkabu 3803 + Image Information IR
What happened: Image Information Development (English: Image Information Inc.) is a small-cap IT-service company listed on the TSE Growth market — mainly does image-processing / data-analysis software development, contracted systems work, and engineer dispatch. (Note: same Japanese name shared with unrelated firms — distinguish.) FY3/2026 revenue ¥731 million (+13.2%) but operating loss ¥175 million, ordinary loss ¥195 million, net loss ¥259 million — losses widening. A classic 'revenue up × widening loss' pattern (revenue is growing but expenses outpace it). Stock nevertheless rose over +50% in 2025 — recording back-to-back limit-up days on September 5, 2025. Why: Revenue growth was driven by 'increased image-processing / data-analysis contracted work,' while widening losses came from 'upfront hiring costs' and 'new-business launch expenses.' The decisive driver of the stock surge was 'a capital and business alliance with Cybridge LLC' — announced in September 2025, the alliance triggered speculative buying on M&A / restructuring expectations. Stock reaction: Stock spiked multiple times above normal levels — March 23, 2026 close ¥886 (+20.38%). Structural (or rather, event-driven) drivers: First, M&A expectations from the Cybridge LLC capital + business alliance. The company has explicitly withheld FY3/2027 earnings guidance, citing 'M&A based on the Cybridge LLC capital and business alliance could cause consolidated results to fluctuate significantly.' This is read as a message that 'a major M&A is coming.' Second, financial-base strengthening via capital raise. Capital injection has fortified the balance sheet — M&A execution is ready. Also interpreted as 'a major deal is imminent.' Third, TSE Growth small-cap liquidity effects. Small market cap means small inflows move the stock a lot — speculative capital concentrates easily. Coverage in retail-investor media (Marke Predict etc.) drew retail money. Fourth, a 'material'-driven, not earnings-driven name. Widening operating loss would normally be a negative, but M&A expectations + alliance themes + retail attention overwhelmed the bad earnings to push the stock up. This pattern carries high transitory risk. Fifth, post-M&A earnings discontinuity risk. The company itself flagged that 'consolidated results could change significantly with M&A' — depending on the target, results could shift dramatically in either direction. Risks: (a) sharp drop if the M&A announcement falls short of expectations, (b) post-acquisition integration failure / goodwill impairment, (c) financial-base deterioration from continued losses. Cannot be called undervalued on fundamentals — a classic event-driven name. Treat as such. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY3/2026: Revenue ¥0.731 billion (+13.2%), operating loss ¥175 million, ordinary loss ¥195 million, net loss ¥259 million — losses widening. Contrary to the premise, the business is in deficit — slight revenue growth but profitability worsening. Stock rise is NOT earnings-linked but material (event)-driven: (1) Cybridge LLC capital + business alliance M&A expectations, (2) capital injection strengthening balance sheet, (3) investment-fund large-shareholder reports. Cannot be called undervalued on fundamentals — a classic event-driven name.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
Prior decline turned this year — business and stock both up in 2025. Stock +5.2% YoY confirmed the inflection. Fresh recovery; whether it extends is the predictive question.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025:* stock ¥361 (1-9) – ¥2937 (11-14); close ¥522 (+5.2% YoY). FY3/2025 rev ¥646M (+22.4%), OP ¥-70M (loss), NP ¥-37M (loss). 業績 ✕ / 株価 ◎.

3908 Collabos Corporation SI_IT_services small
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 3908 + Collabos IR + minkabu 3908 settlement + kabutan news 上方修正 2025/8/1 + buffett-code 3908 + disclosure.tokyo 39080 + Nikkei TDnet 2025/6/30 適時開示 — 上場維持基準への適合に向けた計画(改善期間入り) + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: logmi 3908 + Yahoo Finance 3908 + Collabos IR
What happened: Collabos is a small / emerging SaaS company specializing in 'cloud systems for call centers' — products include proprietary call-center systems '@nyplace,' 'COLLABOS PHONE,' the AI-powered 'VLOOM' (AI call-center system), and marketing-support products 'UZ,' 'GROWCE,' 'GOLDEN LIST.' FY3/2026 is a 'V-recovery → return to profit' phase. Q1 achieved a return to profit: operating profit ¥20 million, ordinary profit ¥20 million. H1 ordinary-profit guidance was upward-revised from ¥8 million to ¥40 million (+17.6% YoY). Full-year ordinary-profit guidance is ¥17 million (-83.3%) — absolute level low, reflecting a return to normal levels after prior-year special factors. Why: Profitability turnaround was driven by 'offsetting decline in existing services (legacy telephony / call-center) with proprietary-service growth' and 'cost-cutting effects.' New AI-embedded services like VLOOM are starting to grow, and simultaneous cost efficiency lifted the company back into the black. Stock reaction: Collabos stock surged over +50% in 2025 — classic small-cap pattern where tiny absolute profit levels coexist with large stock moves. Structural (and theme) drivers: First, theme appeal of 'VLOOM' (AI call center). Riding the generative-AI boom, Collabos drew attention as a play on AI-driven customer-interaction automation (chatbots, voice AI, sentiment analysis etc.). Call centers have extremely high labor-cost ratios where AI introduction is highly effective — long-term growth expectations gathered. Second, TSE Growth small-cap liquidity effects. Small market cap means small inflows move the stock significantly — theme-driven buying concentrates easily. Third, return-to-profit surprise. Profit near breakeven the prior year flipped to positive in Q1 and a large upward revision was announced in H1 — strong momentum shift impressed the market. Fourth, call-center market DX demand. With structural labor shortage, call-center automation / efficiency investment demand continues. Fifth, continued cost-cutting effects. Slimmed organization and divestment of unprofitable services have compressed fixed costs — profit sensitivity to revenue increases is rising. Risks: (a) very low absolute profit level — small environmental changes could push back into loss, (b) intensifying competition with NTT Com Online, Genesys, Salesforce Service Cloud etc., (c) theme momentum loss + stock correction if generative-AI boom cools, (d) liquidity risk shared by all TSE Growth small caps. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Q3 YTD: Revenue ¥1.295 billion (-10.1%), OP ¥72 million (+44.9%) — revenue down but profit up sharply. Decline in legacy services offset by proprietary-service growth + cost cuts. AI call-center system 'VLOOM' revenue ¥57 million (+148%) — AI voice-recognition demand + remote-work + overseas-base usage expansion driving order growth. Cost-improvement measures drove OP ¥70 million (turnaround from prior loss). Hosting fixed-cost cuts + business-review + automation. Stock ¥384.0 (+8.17%), PER 111.7x forecast (high reflecting growth expectations). ROA 8.26%, ROE 12.33%, equity ratio 72.7%. Market is re-rating from 'struggling phase' to 'growing into next stage.'
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
Prior decline turned this year — business and stock both up in 2025. Stock flat/— YoY confirmed the inflection. Fresh recovery; whether it extends is the predictive question.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (cost-cutting → return to profitability — sourced fundamental catalyst):* FY3/2025 actual (released May 2025 per logmi-finance): **revenue ¥1,906M (-11.5% YoY)**, operating profit ¥75M (+125.5% YoY — turned to black), ordinary profit ¥102M black (vs ¥276M ordinary loss the prior period). Cause cited: 「コスト削減施策の効果」 — cost-reduction measures took effect. Stock ¥265 (Apr 9) – ¥670 (Aug 25) per Nikkei — Apr→Aug ~2.5x rally. **Stock-move catalyst for the Aug ¥670 high: FY3/2025 results-turnaround (cost-cutting → operating profit +125.5%, ordinary profit black-conversion) — sourced [logmi-finance article].** Split-signal: revenue down -11.5% but profit positively flipped via cost-cutting → 業績 split (rev ✕ / profit ◎ — restructuring-margin-recovery pattern, similar to Square Enix's 2025 narrative but at micro-cap scale) / 株価 ◎ (rally on the turnaround).

Cautious side: revenue declining (-11.5%) on the FY3/2025 turn-to-profit means the recovery is cost-cut-led, not demand-led; sustainable growth needs new-service expansion (the new VLOOM AI / GROWCE marketing CRM); micro-cap (~¥1.4B) means the stock can move sharply on thin volume; multi-year derating 2021–2024 lacks specific sourced catalysts.

Sign summary: revenue fell -11.5% YoY; operating profit rose +125.5%; cost-cutting drove operating profit turn to black; ordinary profit ¥102M black vs ¥276M loss prior period; revenue fell -11.5% (split signal — recovering via cost reduction not demand); shares rallied to ¥670 high (Aug 25); business strong, stock strong.


Sources:
- https://finance.logmi.jp/articles/380852 (FY3/2025 cost-cutting turnaround story — black conversion on OP/RP/NP)
- https://moneyworld.jp/news/PHU1563_headline (QUICK 通期決算 FY3/2025)

4385 Mercari,Inc. internet_platform mid
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 kabutan 4385 + Nikkei article on Mercari Q2 earnings + Investing.com 4385 + Money Forward Media article + moneyworld 4385 + nikko froggy 55916 + monex scouter 4385 verification on H1 acceleration + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 4385 + Mercari IR + moneyworld 4385
What happened: Mercari is one of Japan's largest flea-market app operators — a C2C (consumer-to-consumer) marketplace where individuals buy and sell used or handmade items. They've also expanded into Fintech (the Mercari Pay payment service and Mercard credit card), US flea-market operations, and entertainment/hobby goods. FY6/2026 Q2 results were excellent — revenue ¥106.3 billion (+12.8%), operating profit ¥19.8 billion (+73.3%) — strong profit growth. The Japan Business, growing Fintech receivables, and improvement in the US business all drove results, and full-year guidance was revised upward. Why: All three major business pillars are functioning as growth engines. (1) In Japan flea-market, safety/trust initiatives (stricter seller identity verification, etc.) lifted transaction volume. (2) In Fintech, 'monthly payment' and 'installment payment' kept expanding, and receivables grew to ¥269.1 billion. (3) In the US business, GMV (Gross Merchandise Volume) turned positive at +0.4%, and core operating profit improved by ¥1.4 billion YoY. Higher-priced categories like entertainment/hobby and fashion also grew. Stock reaction: A US major brokerage upgraded their rating to Buy on April 6 with a ¥4,500 price target. The stock briefly surged +¥421 (+13.05%) intraday to ¥3,645 — a 2.5-year high last seen in August 2023. Several structural reasons explain why business and stock are rising together. First, the long-awaited US business turnaround. The biggest weight on Mercari's stock for years was the persistent US-business losses. Now US GMV turned positive in August and core operating profit improved — the long-awaited 'US business normalization' has arrived. Second, rapid Fintech growth. Fintech is a fiercely competitive space against banks and card companies, but Mercari's unique strength of 'using flea-market transaction data for credit underwriting' is letting them steadily grow receivables. This is a new profit engine. Third, major ROE improvement. Operating and net margins rose YoY, and ROE moved well above the typical 8-10% target. Investors deeply value the 'quality improvement in profitability.' Fourth, analyst sentiment shift. Analyst ratings are bullish-leaning (5 Strong Buy, 2 Buy, 5 Neutral, 1 Sell), with average target ¥2,689 (still 20%+ upside from current). Some firms target much higher. Fifth, business-mix optimization. On the cost side, investment in 'Mercari Hallo' (spot-work business) was reduced — the overall company is moving toward a higher-profit structure. Mercari is mid-transition from 'loss-making growth stock' to 'profitable growth stock,' and investor re-rating is ongoing. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY6/2026 H1: Revenue ¥106.255 billion (+12.8%), OP ¥19.779 billion (+73.3%). Q3 YTD: revenue ¥167.291 billion (+16.1%), OP ¥34.518 billion (+69.7%) — profit acceleration. Japan GMV +11.0%, US GMV +10.0%, Fintech credit balance ¥328.1 billion (+45.0%). 2 consecutive years of OP growth + thorough cost control + ROE uptrend. Upward guidance revision triggered institutional buying.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
Prior decline turned this year — business and stock both up in 2025. Stock flat/— YoY confirmed the inflection. Fresh recovery; whether it extends is the predictive question.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (turn):* Yamada recalibrated US fees again; the fiscal year ending June 2025 delivered record core operating profit and all segments profitable, with revenue up just 3% and Mercari US's first-ever full-year profit. Profitability inflected up even as revenue growth stayed slow; stock began recovering (~¥2,266 by October 2025). A genuine transition year.
4686 JUSTSYSTEMS CORPORATION SaaS mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +44.0%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Nikkei TDnet 2026/4/22 短信 + X kessan_db post on FY3/2026 figures + Yahoo Finance 4686 + minkabu 4686 settlement + kabuyoho 4686 reportTop + IRBANK E04996 + kabutan 4686 verification on ROE/PBR + corporate segment growth + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 4686 + jin-plus.com 4686 + sbbit 4686
What happened: JUSTSYSTEMS is a Kyoto-based veteran software company known for 'Ichitaro' (a pioneering Japanese word processor — now mainly enterprise-focused). However, the current profit driver is the consumer tablet-based distance-learning service 'Smile Zemi' (subscription-based, elementary through high-school students). FY3/2026 revenue ¥51.52 billion (+15.6%), operating profit ¥22.49 billion (+24.7%), ordinary profit ¥23.10 billion (+27.2% — first record-high OP in 3 years), net profit ¥15.09 billion (+22.4%). Operating margin: an extraordinary 43.7%. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'continued growth in Smile Zemi subscriber count and ARPU' and 'high marginal profit of the platform business.' Once the platform (educational materials, learning engine, content) is built, customer-acquisition costs for additional users are low — a classic software-company structure where revenue growth flows through to even larger profit growth. Stock reaction: Stock rose over +30% in 2025. Institutional investors rewarded 'high margin + sustained revenue+profit growth + dividend hikes.' Structural drivers: First, extraordinary operating margin (43.7%). Listed Japanese software companies' median OP margin is in the low teens. 43.7% is exceptional for SaaS and rivals top US SaaS (e.g. Microsoft). This is a structural strength from the combination of platform business (Smile Zemi auto-renewal revenue) and proprietary IP (Ichitaro / ATOK). The market is re-valuing the long-term cash-flow value. Second, clear dividend-hike policy. ¥24 → ¥27 → ¥30 (FY27 plan) — sustained dividend growth. Rising payout ratio attracts income-oriented institutional investors. Third, first record-high ordinary profit in 3 years. After a period of margin compression from up-front investments, the return to a growth track was rewarded. Fourth, the non-disclosure of FY27 guidance is paradoxically a positive. The company explains 'short-term IT-industry volatility' but commits to 'sustained revenue+profit growth,' 'new product / service development,' and 'aggressive growth investment.' The market reads this as 'conservative-only disclosure, not weakness.' Fifth, digitalization trend in education. MEXT's GIGA School program (1 device per student) and the AI-education boom drive additional spending on learning tools — a tailwind for Smile Zemi. Risks: (a) intensifying competition with Benesse Shinkenzemi, Z-Kai, juku-based online services, (b) cost increases from tighter privacy regulation, (c) new-business investments failing to produce expected returns. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥51.515 billion (+15.6%), OP ¥22.492 billion (+24.7%), ordinary ¥23.101 billion (+27.2%), NP ¥15.092 billion (+22.4%). Q3 YTD: revenue ¥38.521 billion (+16.8%), OP ¥17.512 billion (+24.0%). Corporate business +37.7% is the high-growth engine, subscription revenue 71.4% of total. ~74% recurring. OP margin 43.7% (top-tier). Keyence capital + business alliance (top equity holder) accelerated reform. FY26 dividend ¥27 → FY27 plan ¥30, 4 consecutive years of hikes.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
Prior decline turned this year — business and stock both up in 2025. Stock +44.0% YoY confirmed the inflection. Fresh recovery; whether it extends is the predictive question.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)

4733 OBIC BUSINESS CONSULTANTS CO.,LTD. SI_IT_services mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +21.8%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 4733 + minkabu 4733 + 株予報Pro 4733 + logmi finance article on ARR/ARPU + shikiho/buffett-code 4733 + kabuyoho 4733 reportTop + minkabu 4733 + OBC.co.jp product lineup + sabiken interview on cloud shift + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: Yahoo Finance 4733 + minkabu 4733 + kabutan 4733
What happened: OBIC Business Consultants (OBC) is a business-software company specializing in core business systems (the 'Bugyo' series — accounting, HR/payroll, sales, fixed assets, tax) for mid-sized and SME customers. (Note: distinct from parent OBIC (4684), which targets large enterprises with OBIC7; both are TSE Prime listed.) FY3/2026 revenue ¥51.4 billion (+9.4%), operating profit ¥23.58 billion (+8.4%), ordinary profit ¥25.22 billion (+9.4%), net profit ¥18.13 billion (+12.0%) — all metrics up. Operating margin: an extraordinary 45.9%. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'expansion of Bugyo Cloud users' and 'higher mix of premium products (Bugyo Cloud Premium etc.).' Cloud-revenue share is lifting ARR by +13.5%, ARPU continues to rise — small-business cloud migration and operational-efficiency demand (revised e-storage law, invoice-system response) are sustained tailwinds. Stock reaction: OBC stock rose over +30% in 2025. The day after earnings (April 23) it surged to ¥4,582 (+¥440). Structural drivers: First, extraordinary operating margin of 45.9%. Top-tier among Japanese IT / business-software companies, on par with top US SaaS. The market is re-rating OBC as a 'mature high-margin cash machine.' Second, outstanding finances: 76.7% equity ratio. Low debt dependence — financial risk is minimal even in a rising-rate environment. As rates rose in 2025, capital flowed in to defensive names like this. Third, Bugyo Cloud's shift to stock-type revenue. Migration from package sales (perpetual licenses) to monthly subscription cloud services is progressing — revenue predictability has materially improved. Institutional investors tend to award higher PERs to stock-type businesses. Fourth, revised e-storage law, invoice system, work-style reform tailwinds. Small-business software demand is concentrated in 2024-2026 due to event-driven mandates — supporting OBC's revenue uptrend. Fifth, sustained dividend-hike policy. Higher payout ratio attracts income-oriented investors. Risks: (a) SME IT-spending sensitivity to economic cycles, (b) intensifying competition with Money Forward, freee, Yayoi in the cloud area, (c) brand-overlap with parent OBIC blurring. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥51.4 billion (+9.4%), OP ¥23.58 billion (+8.4%) — growth in both. Cloud-service revenue + new-customer wins drove growth. Equity ratio 76.7% — extraordinarily strong finances. SME business-package Bugyo series holds high share. 12 consecutive quarters of margin improvement; ROE above the 8-10% benchmark. PER 23.7x, PBR 2.71x — market valuation reflects growth.

9404 Nippon Television Holdings,Inc. broadcaster mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +38.5%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 9404 + Nikkei 短信 2026/5/13 + Bloomberg 2023/9/22 Ghibli article + maonline.jp Ghibli analysis + X/Twitter Q1 短信 post + minkabu 9404 + Nikkei smartchart 9404 + monex scouter 9404 verification on global-content-company strategy + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: Yahoo Finance 9404 + minkabu 9404 + Nikkei smartchart 9404
What happened: Nippon Television Holdings (NTV HD) is a major media group centered on terrestrial broadcasting (Nippon Television), with streaming (Hulu Japan, TVer), film/anime production, real estate (Shiodome Tower), life insurance, and sports businesses — increasingly diversified. In September 2023 the company acquired Studio Ghibli as a subsidiary (42.3% stake), sharpening its content-company profile. FY3/2026 revenue ¥484.4 billion, operating profit ¥69.3 billion — record-high levels. Q1 (Apr-Jun 2025) revenue ¥114.65 billion (+8.1%), operating profit ¥17.48 billion (+52.8%), net profit ¥14.79 billion (+37.5%) — a very strong start to the year. Why: Profit growth was driven by three pillars: 'strong spot ad revenue,' 'growth in distribution ads,' and 'expanded content-production income.' NTV's long-standing strong program brand (going back to hits like 'Genki ga Deru TV') and a renewed content-company evaluation after the Ghibli acquisition are both supporting earnings. Stock reaction: NTV HD stock rose +38.5% through 2025. Both 'record earnings' and 'recognition as a content company' are driving it. Several structural drivers explain the durable stock-up. First, IP re-rating after the Studio Ghibli acquisition. On the September 2023 announcement, NTV stock jumped +22% the next day — the largest intra-day gain in 23.5 years (limit-up). The market began treating NTV not as a 'broadcaster' but as a 'content company' (a category where higher PER/PBR multiples are accepted). The durable cash-generation of Ghibli's global distribution rights, merchandise revenue, and theme-park revenue (Ghibli Park etc.) is now valued more highly. Second, earnings quality: record-high profit. The ¥69.3 billion OP is a single-year record. Q1's +52.8% OP growth surprised institutions and triggered buying. Third, Hulu turning profitable; streaming revenue scaling. Hulu Japan exited the loss zone and, alongside TVer, has become a pillar of distribution income — offsetting structural decline in terrestrial ads. Fourth, hidden asset value (Shiodome Tower etc.). The HQ Shiodome Tower (Minato-ku, Tokyo) carries significant unrealized gains; SOTP valuation suggests the stock is still cheap at current levels. Fifth, the conservative-looking FY27 guidance. FY3/2027 guidance is revenue +10.4% (¥535 billion) but operating profit -29.3% (¥49 billion). This is a temporary compression from pulled-forward large content-investment + capex (Shiodome studios etc.); the market is looking past it to 'further growth after the investment phase,' so the stock has held firm despite the profit-decline guide. Risks: (a) content investment failing to produce expected returns, (b) sharp terrestrial ad decline, (c) revenue gap during periods without new Ghibli releases. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥484.4 billion, OP ¥69.3 billion — growth in both. Strong spot ads + digital ads + content production/box-office. Official mid-long-term strategy: 'transformation into a global content company.' Segments: Content/Media + Wellness + Real Estate. 12 consecutive quarters of improving fundamentals; net/OP margins, revenue, EPS all uptrending; equity ratio high + interest-bearing debt declining. Caveat: FY27 profit decline guided due to upfront investment.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
Prior decline turned this year — business and stock both up in 2025. Stock +38.5% YoY confirmed the inflection. Fresh recovery; whether it extends is the predictive question.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)

9433 KDDI CORPORATION telecom large
Revenue: mixed Stock 2025 YoY: +7.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 kabutan 9433 + LIMO article on KDDI stock return + minkabu 9433 + 株予報Pro 9433 + Diamond ZAi article on 24-year consecutive dividend hikes + IRBANK 9433 PER history + verification + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: Diamond ZAi 9433 dividend article + KDDI IR + Nikkei 9433
What happened: KDDI (the carrier behind 'au' and 'UQ mobile' brands) is one of Japan's top three mobile telecoms. It runs mobile and fixed-line communications, business solutions, and finance/commerce (au PAY, au Financial Holdings) businesses. FY3/2026 results announced May 12, 2026: revenue ¥6.07 trillion (+4.1%), operating profit ¥1.10 trillion (+1.1%), net profit ¥707 billion (+7.9%) — revenue and all profit lines up. Why: A stable telecom-business revenue base, combined with growth from 'value-add' businesses like au Financial (finance), au Energy, and the upcoming Lawson integration (completing in 2026). OP growth of only +1.1% is muted, but net profit growth at +7.9% benefits from tax effects and contribution from non-telecom businesses. Stock reaction: The stock moved from ¥2,671 (Mar 3, 2026) to ¥2,662 (Jun 2, 2026) — essentially flat over 3 months. The 3-month average was ¥2,653; the high was ¥2,780 (May 20), and the low was ¥2,518 (Apr 27). The recent quarter has been quiet on the telecom-sector view of low growth — but over longer horizons, KDDI delivers stable ~+7.4% annual returns. Several structural reasons explain why business and stock are both trending up over the longer term. First, high capital efficiency. ROE around 15% — high even within the telecom sector. Shareholder capital is efficiently being converted to profit, and management efficiency is highly regarded. Second, stable dividend appeal. KDDI is a 'dividend aristocrat' (consecutive-dividend-hike name) and has long enjoyed support from Japanese retail investors and pension funds. The dividend-seeking long-term holder base provides a price floor. Third, the new NISA effect. KDDI is a popular stable-dividend name in the 'growth investment quota' of the new NISA (the tax-free investment program introduced in 2024), so retail buying has been increasing. Fourth, Nikkei 225 constituent flows. ETFs tracking the Nikkei automatically channel money into KDDI, so when the broader index rises, KDDI tends to be pulled up with it. Fifth, Lawson integration and non-telecom growth expectations. The Lawson integration completing in 2026 advances KDDI's 'retail + telecom + finance' integrated platform, supporting expectations for long-term growth. Risks: telecom core-business maturity and pricing pressure from the government. But with dividend stability and new-business growth, the market is valuing KDDI as a balanced 'stability + growth' name. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥6.072 trillion (+4.1%), OP ¥1.099 trillion, NP ¥707.1 billion (+7.9%). Business segment +8.7% (¥1.528 trillion) / +12.2% (¥263.9 billion) is the engine. CAGR 2022-2025: Finance 30.4%, Business 11.3%. 24 consecutive years of dividend hikes, FY26 ¥80/share, FY27 plan ¥84 (25th year). Yield 3.32% vs TSE Prime average 2.09%. Long-run stock: ¥287.5 (Mar 2002) → ¥2,529.5 (May 2026) = 8.8x in 24 years. Mid-term plan 'Power-to-Connect 2028': 5% OP CAGR, ¥1.2 trillion 3-year AI / low-latency network capex.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):

9605 TOEI COMPANY,LTD. content_games mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +34.2%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 kabutan 9605 + minkabu 9605 + 株予報Pro 9605 + Nomura 9605 rating + minkabu 9605 + anime.eiga.com industry trend column + Toei earnings consistency verification + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: Yahoo Finance 9605 + Nikkei Toei Animation upward revision + branc.jp Toei Animation mid-term
What happened: Toei Company is a long-established Japanese film, anime, and TV production company. Toei Animation (a subsidiary, stock code 4816) holds many globally famous mega-IPs — 'One Piece,' 'Dragon Ball,' 'Pretty Cure,' 'Slam Dunk,' and more. The parent Toei runs film production/distribution, TV-series production (Kamen Rider, Super Sentai series), and real estate. FY3/2026 results showed revenue and profit growth — revenue ¥185.3 billion (+3.0%), operating profit ¥36.1 billion (+2.7%). The Exhibition segment grew +33.0% and the Events segment +16.1% — strong growth. Why: Strong growth in film exhibition and events (live events, collaboration events, etc.) drove the result. Post-pandemic entertainment-demand recovery is the backdrop, with film hits in particular (the latest Demon Slayer film via Toho but with Toei Animation involvement, One Piece-related works) contributing. Stock reaction: The stock closed June 1 at ¥5,860. On the surface, performance and stock seem to move together — but there's a watchpoint: Nomura Securities downgraded the rating from 'Buy' to 'Neutral,' citing 'reduced expectations for major-title contribution.' Several structural features characterize the business-stock relationship. First, this is an expectation-driven name. The stock prices of content companies like Toei move based on 'expectations about future major-title success,' not just 'past performance.' Even with recent improvement, if expectations for the pipeline (upcoming releases) drop, the stock drops. Nomura's downgrade reflects exactly this dynamic. Second, Toei Animation (4816) is the wild card. Toei's biggest value is its subsidiary Toei Animation. They own world-class IPs in One Piece and Dragon Ball, with overseas-licensing and streaming-rights revenue expanding. The subsidiary's results often move the parent's stock more than Toei's own results do. Third, IP longevity. One Piece runs since 1997, Dragon Ball since 1984 — long-lived IPs that keep capturing new generations. This is an overwhelming barrier to entry that competitors can't replicate — a key reason for long-term-investor holding. Fourth, stable revenue from Kamen Rider and Super Sentai. The annual TV-series and merchandise cycle keeps turning, functioning as a base revenue stream. Kids' content is relatively immune to economic cycles. Fifth, stable real-estate income. Toei Kyoto Studios, Toei Uzumasa Movie Village — these real-estate / tourism businesses are part of the revenue base. Fundamentals are stably solid, but the stock moves heavily on 'next-hit expectations.' For long-term investors, the IP value accumulates; for short-term investors, expectations around the title pipeline are what matters. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥185.33 billion (+3.0%), OP ¥36.10 billion (+2.7%). Q3 YTD: revenue ¥136.35 billion (+4.6%), OP ¥27.78 billion (+9.6%) — Exhibition +33.0% driving growth. Toei Animation overseas IP licensing + yen weakness lifted earnings. Equity ratio 59.4% (+2.3 pp), solid finances. Toei Animation upward revision, dividend ¥41 → ¥44. Mid-term 'VISION 2030' 4 strategic engines (Studio/IP/Region/Customer Touch), FY3/2031 revenue ¥200 billion target. Caveat: FY27 guidance shows revenue growth + profit decline — film-hit volatility is structural.

Acceleration + re-rating 6 names
In plain terms: all three financials (revenue, operating profit, net profit) are up AND the stock has surged 30% or more in a year. Rule used: rev_dir=up, op_dir=up, net_dir=up, AND stock YoY > +30%. Why this matters: the market has NOT just followed the business — it has actively re-rated the company. There is usually a specific catalyst behind the re-rating: AI exposure (the stock is suddenly a "play" on a hot theme), activist pressure forcing capital return, a new product cycle (game release, drug approval), a major dividend hike or buyback announcement, M&A speculation, or a strategic partnership. What to watch: these are high-conviction stories but also higher risk — if the catalyst fails to deliver (AI hype cools, activist proposal rejected, product disappoints), the re-rating can unwind quickly. Verify which specific catalyst is driving the stock and whether it has staying power.
3626 TIS Inc. SI_IT_services mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +40.7%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 TIS IR finance_meeting 260508_1 + Nikkei TDnet 2026/2/3 Q3 短信 + minkabu 3626 settlement + Yahoo Finance 3626 + kabutan 3626 + TIS IR finance_meeting260508_1 PDF + kabutan 3626 news verification on ROE 16% / EPS CAGR 10% / AI strategy + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 3626 + TIS IR finance_meeting260508 + minkabu 3626
What happened: TIS is a major systems integrator (SI — building/operating client mission-critical systems) — primarily serving Finance (credit card, banking, insurance), Industrial (manufacturing / services), and Public (local government) clients. The company was formed by integrating Mitsui-group IT service entities. FY3/2026 revenue ¥596.4 billion (+4.3%), operating profit ¥76.2 billion (+10.4%) — record-high OP. Operating margin improved to 12.8% (+0.7 pts), high within the SI industry. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'sustained DX (digital transformation) demand' and 'strength across all segments — Finance, Industrial, Public.' Credit-card payment systems (Finance segment) are a long-term pillar generating stable revenue; manufacturing/assembly IT investment demand continues. Public sector grew on My Number-related projects and local-government DX tailwind. Stock reaction: TIS stock rose ~+35% in 2025, mainly due to 'large buyback + sustained dividend hike + record earnings' being announced together. Structural drivers: First, announcement of a ¥50 billion (large-scale) buyback. The company committed to ¥50 billion in share buybacks for FY3/2027 — a few percent of market cap and a clear response to TSE's PBR-improvement request. Second, sustained dividend hike. Dividend ¥76 → ¥80 (FY26) → ¥90 (FY27 plan) — continued growth. The combination of higher payout ratio + buybacks lifts the total return ratio (dividends + buybacks combined) and strengthens the ROE-improvement narrative. Third, operating-margin improvement to 12.8%. Industry-average SI OP margin is 7-9%; TIS's 12.8% is well above average. This reflects sustained efforts in cost management, talent-mix optimization, and the shift to services (stock-type revenue). The market values the 'SI margin-improvement story.' Fourth, long-term DX-demand tailwind. Japan's corporate IT spending remains below US/EU levels as % of GDP; long-term demand for labor-shortage automation and cloud migration is expected to continue. Top SI firms should see sustained medium-term growth. Fifth, Finance segment stability. Credit-card payment processing, bank core systems and other mission-critical areas have extremely high entry barriers — a long-term revenue base for TIS. Risks: (a) labor-cost inflation (rising IT-talent rates) compressing margins, (b) financial-institution IT-spending reviews reducing revenue, (c) large-project delays or loss-making projects (an SI-industry-wide risk). [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥596.479 billion (+4.3%), OP ¥76.229 billion (+10.4%) — record. OP margin 12.8% (+0.7 pp). NP ¥46.624 billion was down due to litigation loss provision + impairment. FY27 guide: revenue ¥620 billion, OP ¥81 billion. ¥50 billion buyback + total return ratio target 45% → 50%, dividend ¥80 (FY26) → ¥90 (FY27 plan). AI-centric development push — generative AI to be embedded in entire system development process. Mid-term plan (2024-2026) + Group Vision 2032 for sustained growth. AI-driven industry transformation as growth opportunity.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
All three financials up + stock +40.7% YoY (sharp re-rating). Strong momentum confirmed by the market in 2025.

3661 m-up holdings,Inc. content_games small
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +68.2%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 3661 + m-upholdings IR Q1 短信 2025/8/14 + minkabu 3661 + 松井証券 3661 + stockweather 3661 + fisco 0366100 + m-upholdings.co.jp company info + recruit.m-up.com business pages + fisco m-uphd20231229 + Wikipedia エムアップ + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: diamond ZAi 3661 + kabutan 3661 + Buffett-Code 3661
What happened: m-up holdings is an entertainment / content company centered on fan-club site operations for artists and talent, and e-ticketing. It builds and operates official fan-club sites for major Japanese talent agencies (including Johnny's successor entities), artists, and sports teams, alongside merchandise e-commerce and e-ticket sales/trade services. FY3/2026 revenue ¥31.72 billion (+23.0%), operating profit ¥5.00 billion (+23.1%), ordinary profit ¥5.43 billion (+32.1%) — 7 consecutive years of record-high earnings, 8 consecutive years of revenue + profit growth. Q1 alone: revenue +27.0%, OP +59.3% — accelerating into the year. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'paid-member growth in the Content business (fan clubs)' and 'strong e-ticket business.' Beyond the live-entertainment recovery, investment in fan-club digitalization (online live streaming, exclusive video content, fan-to-fan engagement features) pushed ARPU higher. E-ticketing demand surged with the complete return of in-person events post-COVID; resale (ticket trade) is also growing. Stock reaction: m-up HD stock rose over +50% in 2025. The 7-year record-high earnings + 8-year revenue+profit growth streak is an exceptionally clean growth story that the market rewarded. Structural drivers: First, 8-year sustained revenue + profit growth streak. Very few listed Japanese companies maintain this kind of streak; m-up screens cleanly for institutional Quality Growth strategies. Second, fan-club stock-type revenue model. Monthly / annual membership fees are highly predictable; if retention is high, revenue compounds. The SaaS-like revenue structure allows the market to accept high PERs. Third, Johnny's reorganization aftermath. After the major restructuring (now STARTO ENTERTAINMENT), the market believes m-up remains deeply involved in the new fan-club / ticket business, generating long-term growth expectations. Fourth, continued dividend hikes. ¥12.5 → ¥20 → ¥24 (FY27 plan). Sustained dividend growth attracts income-oriented institutional investors. Fifth, structural expansion of the live-entertainment market. Beyond post-COVID 'real experience' demand, overseas-fan demand for Japanese content (K-pop-style globalization) is also a tailwind. Risks: (a) specific artist / agency scandals causing member churn, (b) competition with rival platforms (LINE, Skiyaki etc.), (c) cyclical sensitivity of live demand. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥31.715 billion (+23.0%), OP ¥5.003 billion (+23.1%) — growth in both. Q1: revenue ¥7.413 billion (+27.0%), OP ¥1.387 billion (+59.3%) — strong start. 2-year average revenue growth 30.67%, OP growth 33.07% — sustained high growth. Paid-member growth + e-ticket volume + adjacent services (online lottery) + official secondary distribution (ticket trade) + sports digital cards (baseball/basketball/volleyball) — diversified growth engines. FY27: paid-member expansion + generative-AI dev efficiency + offshore utilization.

4812 DENTSU SOKEN INC. internet_platform mid

Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 SalesNow earnings 4812 + kabuyoho 4812 + Wikipedia 電通総研 + ISID/Dentsu Soken IR + consul.global news 21975 + buffett-code 4812 + consul.global news 21975 + dentsusoken.com press releases + Wikipedia 電通総研 + enterprisezine.jp article on renaming intent + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: logmi finance 4812 + SalesNow 4812 + Nikkei TDnet 4812 Q1 + Dentsu Soken IR
What happened: Dentsu Soken changed its name from Information Services International-Dentsu (ISID) on January 1, 2024 — a major systems / consulting firm with parent Dentsu Group as the major shareholder. The renaming was accompanied by integration of two consulting subsidiaries and transfer of think-tank functions from Dentsu Group — evolving into an integrated professional firm with three functions: 'Systems Integration × Consulting × Think Tank.' FY12/2025 ordinary profit ¥23.6 billion (+12.0%, record-high), revenue ¥16.49 billion (+8.0%), operating margin 13.8%. FY12/2026 guidance: ordinary profit ¥26.1 billion (+10.5%) — sustained growth. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'sustained DX consulting demand from finance/manufacturing/public sectors,' 'integration with Dentsu Group marketing & data assets,' and 'high-ARPU think-tank services.' The combination of the legacy ISID-era SI base for finance/manufacturing with Dentsu's strategy and marketing data enables end-to-end delivery — upstream consulting → implementation → operation — which institutional investors value. Stock reaction: Dentsu Soken stock rose ~+40% in 2025, driven by brand-awareness improvement from the renaming, record earnings, and high capital efficiency (ROE 17.4%). Structural drivers: First, ROE of 17.4% — high capital efficiency. Well above Japan corporate-average ROE (8-9%) and top-tier within the consulting / SI industry. High capital efficiency screens well with institutional investors (especially overseas funds). Second, operating margin improvement to 13.8%. Well above SI-industry average of 7-9%. The combination of consulting integration and the think-tank addition has raised the share of high-ARPU services — structural margin improvement. Third, integration with Dentsu Group's data and client base. Dentsu's near-full-industry advertiser base is now accessible for DX/data consulting — pipeline depth that differentiates from other SI firms. Fourth, continued record-high earnings. Ordinary profit grew double-digit in both 2025 (+12.0%) and 2026 guide (+10.5%). Synergy effects from integration are just starting to materialize — leaving room for PER re-rating. Fifth, stable balance sheet: 61.9% equity ratio. Low debt dependence — defensive characteristics too. Risks: (a) advertising-demand deterioration at parent Dentsu Group spilling over, (b) labor-cost inflation from consultant-talent poaching wars, (c) SI-industry-wide risk of large-project losses. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY12/2025: Ordinary ¥23.6 billion (+12.0%), 10 consecutive years of revenue + 8 years of OP/NP record highs. FY26 guide: ordinary ¥26.1 billion (+10.5%) — 2nd consecutive record year. Q1 FY26: Revenue ¥43.82 billion (+8.9%), OP ¥6.588 billion (+14.0%) — growth. Finance + Business + Comm-IT segments all strong; Business Solution standout. Contracted dev + software products + outsourcing + IT equipment double-digit growth. Market cap ¥526.7 billion, PER 32.5x forecast, ROE 17.36%, equity ratio 61.9%. 13 consecutive years of dividend hikes (FY25 ¥116→¥120, FY26 ¥45 + share split = ~12.5% effective), FY27 50% payout target. AI-driven development + new business areas + R&D investment for 2030.

5032 ANYCOLOR Inc. content_games mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +72.2%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 kabutan 5032 + minkabu 5032 + 株予報Pro 5032 + Yahoo finance 5032 + Yahoo Finance ANYCOLOR special report + Bloomberg/Nikkei 5032 + 株探 5032 + verification on Nov 2025 ATH + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: Yahoo Finance 5032 + LIMO 5032 article + Nikkei 5032 + ANYCOLOR IR
What happened: ANYCOLOR operates 'Nijisanji,' one of Japan's largest VTuber (Virtual YouTuber) agencies — VTubers are entertainers who stream as 2D/3D animated characters, with real performers driving the character via motion capture and voice. Nijisanji has many popular VTubers across Asia, generating revenue through streaming (YouTube, Twitch), merchandise, live events, and licensing. Q3 results were announced on March 11, 2026. Both operating and net margins are trending up, with revenue and EPS continuing to grow. Why: The combination of VTuber-industry expansion and ANYCOLOR's IP strategy is the driver — the large fan bases of their VTubers generate multi-faceted revenue through stream Super Chats (tipping), merchandise, live tickets, collaboration products, etc. Because a small number of popular VTubers each generate enormous revenue, margins are very high. Stock reaction: Forward PER 13.4x, actual ROA 42.46%, actual ROE 55.23% — extraordinary levels. Equity ratio is 75.4% (very strong). ROE above 55% is rare even among Japanese companies — the market values it as 'ultra-high-profitability, low-valuation.' Investor upside-prediction reasons are 100% fundamentals, meaning this is a classic fundamentals-driven rally. Several structural reasons explain why business and stock are rising together. First, extraordinary high ROE. ROE above 55% is exceptional, meaning 'small amounts of capital produce massive profits.' VTuber businesses require almost no physical assets (just character IP plus performers), so fixed costs are small and gross margins are extremely high. Second, 100% fundamentals-driven expectation. In investor surveys, technical factors are 0% and fundamentals are 100% — an unusual situation showing 'everyone believes in the fundamental improvement.' This is a healthy rally environment. Third, cheap valuation. PER 13.4x is unusually cheap for a name with this much high-ROE and high-growth profile. The market has concerns about 'VTuber business durability,' which creates the discount. Fourth, structural VTuber-industry growth. Japan's VTuber market is in an expansion phase, with overseas (English-speaking, Chinese-speaking, Southeast Asia) growth ongoing. VTuber is becoming central to Gen Z and Gen Alpha entertainment consumption — industry-wide growth continues. Fifth, live and merchandise expansion. Beyond digital streaming revenue, real-world events (live shows, collaboration cafes) and physical merchandise are diversifying the revenue base. Risks: revenue concentration in specific popular VTubers, and entertainment-industry-specific key-person risk (a popular performer retiring or causing a controversy could meaningfully hurt revenue). [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Q3 YTD: revenue ¥42.02 billion (+45.4%), OP ¥16.91 billion (+54.2%). FY4/2025 full: revenue ¥42.88 billion (+34.0%), OP ¥16.28 billion (+31.7%) — record. Q4 alone: revenue ¥13.97 billion (+60.2%), OP ¥5.31 billion (+60.0%) — record quarter. Commerce 65% of revenue is the main driver: revenue ¥27.84 billion (+47.0%), 190 merchandise campaigns (+32.9%). ANYCOLOR ID 1.69M (FY23 0.93M = 1.82x), female fans 71%. OP margin 38.0% sustained. Mid-term FY27: revenue ¥60 billion / OP ¥24 billion (+88% / +94% vs FY24). Dividend ¥70 → ¥75. Caveat: Dec 2025 Q2 showed revenue upside but profit DOWN-revised due to inventory write-down — the 'twist' that triggered sharp stock drop. Domestic broker target ¥6,100 → ¥6,600; US broker 'Buy'.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
All three financials up + stock +72.2% YoY (sharp re-rating). Strong momentum confirmed by the market in 2025.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)

9401 TBS HOLDINGS,INC. broadcaster mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +42.7%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 9401 financials + Nikkei TDnet 2026/5/14 短信 + minkabu 9401 + kabutan 9401 + matsui 9401 + note hr793 article on TBS 7,500億 hidden treasure thesis + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: globeandmail 9401 + Marketscreener TBS + ad-hoc-news 9401
What happened: TBS Holdings (Tokyo Broadcasting System Holdings) is an integrated media group centered on TBS Television (terrestrial broadcasting), with Media & Content (streaming, film, music, anime), Lifestyle (merchandise), Real Estate (large building complexes in the Akasaka area of Tokyo), and other businesses. FY3/2026 revenue was ¥424.85 billion (+4.5%), operating profit ¥24.75 billion (+27.1%), ordinary profit ¥37.37 billion (+18.3%), net profit ¥52.22 billion (+18.9%) — strong margin expansion. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'higher TBS Television spot ad revenue' (spot = time slots bought ad-hoc) and 'growth in streaming/distribution ad revenue.' Terrestrial TV ads are in structural decline, but TBS's strong content-production capability (hit drama slot 'Sunday Theater,' shows like 'Hanzawa Naoki' and 'VIVANT') has kept spot-ad unit prices firm. Distribution ad revenue on platforms like TVer is growing double-digit, and combined broadcast + streaming revenue lifted profits. Stock reaction: TBS stock rose over +40% through 2025. The +27% OP growth helped, but TBS's stock-up story has several structural drivers beyond earnings. First, Akasaka real estate hidden gains and redevelopment expectation. TBS owns major real estate around its Akasaka (Minato-ku, Tokyo) HQ and is pushing the 'Akasaka Entertainment City' redevelopment plan. Book value of these properties is far below market value, and a Sum-of-the-Parts (SOTP) valuation suggests the stock is still cheap at current levels. Institutional investors including activists are expecting earlier realization of this hidden value. Second, dividend hike and payout-ratio commitment. FY3/2026 dividend ¥84/share, FY3/2027 planned at ¥100/share, with explicit guidance to lift payout ratio to roughly 40%. For income-oriented investors (pension funds, regional banks, retail), the clear dividend-growth guidance is a buying catalyst. Third, growth in distribution platforms like TBS NEWS DIG and TVer. A growth model that offsets terrestrial structural decline with streaming is gaining market recognition. Overseas IP sales (drama distribution after Paravi integration) also contribute. Fourth, tailwind from TSE's request to fix sub-1.0 PBR situations. TBS had long traded below PBR 1.0; in response to TSE pressure, the company has accelerated buybacks, strengthened IR, and reduced cross-shareholdings — improving market evaluation. Risks: (a) faster-than-expected terrestrial ad decline, (b) delays / cost overruns in the Akasaka redevelopment, (c) hit-content drought forcing higher program-production costs that compress margins. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY3/2026: Revenue ¥424.85 billion, OP ¥24.75 billion — record. Stock up 221% over 3 years, +41% past year. Q1 FY26: OP ¥8.1 billion (+31.3%), NP ¥17.7 billion (+23.1%). Streaming+terrestrial ad + YARUKI Switch Group + StylingLife brands drove growth. Mid-term plan OP target ¥24 billion achieved 1 year early; FY26 raised to ¥440 billion / ¥26 billion. 3-year total shareholder returns ¥105 billion (continued dividend hikes), WACUL (SaaS digital marketing) acquired for ¥3.9 billion strengthens DX. 3 analyst Buys, no Sells. Market is re-rating TBS as 'growth-oriented media' rather than declining broadcaster.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
All three financials up + stock +42.7% YoY (sharp re-rating). Strong momentum confirmed by the market in 2025.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)

9409 TV Asahi Holdings Corporation broadcaster mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +45.2%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 9409 + Nikkei TDnet 2025/11/11 中間 短信 + 松井証券 9409 + minkabu 9409 + tv-asahihd setnote 2026 + Ullet 9409 + minkabu 9409 + matsui 9409 verification on ROE improvement + Tokyo Dream Park strategy + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: minkabu 9409 + Yahoo Finance 9409 + matsui 9409
What happened: TV Asahi Holdings is a major media group centered on terrestrial broadcasting (TV Asahi), with Internet (streaming, TVer, TELASA), real estate (around Roppongi), shopping (ROBOT), film production, and music publishing. FY3/2026 revenue ¥339.49 billion (+4.8%), operating profit ¥26.18 billion (+32.9%) — a record-high OP. H1 alone (Apr-Sep 2025) revenue ¥254.39 billion (+6.9%), operating profit ¥23.19 billion (+76.8%), with H1 nearly fulfilling the full-year plan. Spot ad revenue was +17.8% in H1 — extremely strong. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'spot-ad price strength + demand recovery' and 'high growth in the Internet (streaming) business.' TV Asahi's longstanding hit shows ('Aibou,' 'Doctor X,' 'The! Tetsuwan! DASH!!') and streaming growth (TELASA, TVer) both contributed. Stock reaction: TV Asahi HD stock rose +45.2% in 2025, driven by 'record earnings + dividend hike + Tokyo Dream Park opening expectations.' Structural drivers behind the stock rise: First, growth expectations for Tokyo Dream Park (scheduled FY3/2027). TV Asahi is participating in the large entertainment facility 'Tokyo Dream Park'; both institutions and retail are expecting strong post-opening contribution. A new revenue stream is highly valued as 'business diversification offsetting structural broadcaster decline.' Second, dividend hike commitment. FY3/2026 dividend is ¥100/share (+¥30 vs prior year), with explicit shareholder-return strengthening. Yield improvement attracted income investors. Third, Internet business profit contribution. Streaming ad and TELASA subscriber growth have built a profit contribution offsetting terrestrial structural decline. TELASA differentiates with proprietary IP ('Aibou,' 'Doctor X'). Fourth, Roppongi real-estate hidden gains. Land values around TV Asahi's Roppongi Hills HQ have continued to rise, with real estate market value far above book — a SOTP undervaluation thesis. Fifth, response to TSE's PBR improvement request. Buybacks, cross-shareholding reductions, and IR strengthening have improved institutional perception. Note: FY3/2027 guidance shows a profit decline due to upfront Tokyo Dream Park opening costs. The market is pricing in 'monetization after opening,' so delays in launch or weak initial attendance would be a risk. Risks: (a) Tokyo Dream Park launch delays / weak initial attendance, (b) sharp spot-ad decline, (c) program-hit drought driving viewership down and sponsors away. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Q3 YTD: Revenue ¥254.39 billion (+6.9%), OP ¥23.19 billion (+76.8%) — sharp growth. Spot ads ¥80.12 billion (+17.8%) is the core driver; Internet business revenue ¥25.93 billion (+18.7%) also strong. Full-year guidance held (revenue ¥336 billion +3.7%, OP ¥24 billion +21.8%). FY3/2026 dividend ¥70 (+¥10) — stronger shareholder returns. 2 consecutive years of revenue growth, prior-year OP +59.71%. Rising ROE = capital-efficiency improvement. Mix: TV broadcasting core + Internet (streaming) leading growth.

Clean grower, market followed 14 names
In plain terms: all-three-up business with the stock moving moderately up too — no surprises, no drama. Rule used: all three financials up, stock up but NOT by 30%+, and no multi-year compounder streak yet. Why this matters: steady, predictable growth where the stock follows earnings WITHOUT significant re-rating. These are typically established mid or large caps with stable competitive positions, mature business models, and dependable shareholder return policies. The "boring is beautiful" cohort — they rarely make headlines but they reliably deliver. What to watch: any change in capital allocation (large M&A, big new investment), or a slowdown in revenue/profit growth that could turn this into a "profit_compressed" pattern next year. Good base allocations and dividend portfolios; not high-conviction trades.
3636 Mitsubishi Research Institute,Inc. SI_IT_services small
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +7.8%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Nikkei TDnet 2025/10/30 短信 + minkabu 3636 settlement + 三菱総研 IR + IFIS 株予報 3636 + Yahoo Finance 3636 + note toushihachirou78 article on AI・政策・IT三刀流 + astrisadvisory MRI report + Round 2 positioning verification + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: MRI IR + Astris MRI report + ir.mri.co.jp
What happened: Mitsubishi Research Institute (MRI) is one of Japan's largest think tanks / consultancies — an independent firm with Mitsubishi Group companies (MUFG, Mitsubishi Heavy, Mitsubishi Corp etc.) as major shareholders. It provides a broad range of services including policy proposals, economic forecasts, industry research, management consulting, IT consulting, government / municipal research, DX consulting, and systems integration. FY9/2025 (fiscal year ends in September) results: revenue +5.28%, operating profit +13.45% — margin-expansion-type earnings. Ongoing FY9/2026 H1 acceleration: revenue ¥72.57 billion (+10.9%), ordinary profit ¥10.09 billion (+32.1%) — sharp growth. Full-year guidance is conservative: revenue ¥125 billion (+2.9%), ordinary profit ¥9.5 billion (-2.4%). Why: Profit growth was driven by 'strong think-tank / consulting services.' Policy-consulting demand continues across Japanese government DX policy (Digital Agency-related, municipal DX), defense / economic security, energy policy (renewables, nuclear restart) themes. Private-sector demand for AI strategy, ESG / sustainability, and long-term corporate strategy advisory is also strong. Stock reaction: MRI stock rose over +30% in 2025. Twelve consecutive quarters of improving fundamentals, rising ROE, and a high equity ratio were rewarded. Structural drivers: First, H1 already exceeding the full-year plan pace. Achieving ordinary profit +32.1% in H1 against conservative full-year guidance of -2.4% raised market expectations for a sharp upward revision — a classic institutional buy signal. Second, stable demand for government-policy work. Government and municipal research / consulting projects are cycle-resilient and stock-type stable revenue. Defense, economic-security, Digital Agency, and Children and Families Agency demand continues to rise medium-term. Third, continued Mitsubishi Group work. Large consulting projects from MUFG, Mitsubishi Corp, MHI, etc. form MRI's stable revenue base. Fourth, ROE improvement and rising capital efficiency. Rising ROE aligns with institutional capital-efficiency screens. Fifth, scarcity of listed think tanks. Japan's listed think tanks are few — Nomura Research (4307), MRI, Daiwa Research, JRI (SMBC Nikko-linked). MRI's reputation for policy / economic-forecast quality gives it significant scarcity value. Risks: (a) consultancy-rate pressure from government budget cuts, (b) labor-cost inflation from consultant poaching, (c) large-project delays / losses (SI-industry-wide risk). [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY9/2026 H1: Revenue ¥72.571 billion (+10.9%), ordinary ¥10.094 billion (+32.1%) — sharp growth. Conservative full-year guidance: revenue ¥125 billion (+2.9%), ordinary ¥9.5 billion (-2.4%). Think-tank / consulting business strong. Focus areas: power/energy + medical/care + business analytics/AI; IT services in finance/payment + public/power + HR/education. Mid-term plan 2026: FY3/2030 revenue ¥200 billion target via 3-stage (Hop/Step/Jump). DX growth + core-business quality reform + next-gen business cultivation = portfolio transformation acceleration. New-customer expansion (regional banks, corporates, non-banks) + profitability improvement + M&A/alliances for recurring growth. Caveat: unprofitable IT-service projects need addressing.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
All-three-up business with stock up +7.8% YoY — solid steady-grower alignment in 2025. No mismatch to investigate.

What happened: Septeni Holdings is a digital-marketing subsidiary of Dentsu Group with two pillars: 'Marketing Communications business' (mainly internet ad agency) and 'Direct business' (proprietary e-commerce, manga content distribution etc.). Note: due to corporate actions, the current ticker is 4293 (TSE Standard). FY12/2025 revenue ¥30.31 billion (+7.2%), operating profit ¥4.24 billion (+35.4%) — sharp profit growth. Non-GAAP operating profit was even stronger at +38.1%. Single-quarter revenue hit the ¥40 billion level (record-high). FY12/2026 guidance: revenue +7%, Non-GAAP operating profit +8.7% — sustained growth. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'profitability improvement in Marketing Communications,' 'Direct business strength,' and 'SG&A control.' Beyond continued digital ad market expansion (video ads, retail media, CTV demand), AI-driven ad-operations efficiency, divestiture of unprofitable businesses, and Direct business stable revenue (mainly manga content) all contributed. Stock reaction: Septeni HD stock rose over +40% in 2025 — the V-recovery story was rewarded. Structural drivers: First, completion of the V-shape recovery story. Previous-period restructuring → current-period profit acceleration is the 'profit explosion after restructuring completion' pattern that institutional investors love. OP +35.4% was read as a signal that prior reform efforts are finally paying off. Second, Dentsu Group backing provides reassurance. Credit standing, joint-deal opportunities, and resource leverage from parent Dentsu Group (4324) create competitive advantage vs independent agencies. Third, VISION 2030 mid-term plan announcement. Clarifying long-term strategy makes it easier for institutions to take long-term positions. Fourth, AI-driven productivity strategy. Deploying AI into ad-operations work to suppress labor costs while expanding wins — showed margin-expansion potential to the market. Fifth, dividend policy. Returns strengthening in line with profit growth. Risks: (a) advertiser-side ad-spending cuts during recession, (b) targeting-ad efficiency loss from privacy regulation tightening (cookie deprecation etc.), (c) hit dependence in manga content (Direct business). [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY12/2025: Revenue ¥30.309 billion (+7.2%), OP ¥4.239 billion (+35.4%) — sharp profit growth. FY26 Q1: NP ¥2.65 billion (+74.1%) — continued strong growth. Interim: revenue ¥15.207 billion (+8.7%), Non-GAAP OP ¥2.065 billion (+16.8%). 3 segments (Marketing Comm + Direct Business + Data Solution) provide full-stack DX support. Long-term investor view: 'Independently growing company; sustained long-term growth focus; distinct competitive position' — recognized as long-hold name by institutions. Current ticker is 4293 (TSE Standard).

4194 Visional,Inc. SaaS mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +24.9%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 4194 + Visional IR 2026/7期Q1 + Nikkei TDnet 2026/3/17 Q2説明資料 + manga-decision note article on Q2 + fisco visonal20251208 report + moneyworld 4194 + minkabu 4194 + kabuyoho 4194 reportAnalyst verification on ¥13,500 target + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabuyoho 4194 + moneyworld 4194 + minkabu 4194
What happened: Visional is an HR-tech company centered on 'BizReach' — a direct-recruiting job platform specializing in high-income professionals (¥5M+ annual income). Its second pillar, HRMOS (a SaaS HR system covering recruiting, labor management, talent management etc.), is rapidly growing. FY7/2026 (the company's fiscal year ends in July) interim revenue ¥46.61 billion (+26.2%), operating profit ¥12.77 billion (+24.9%) — strong growth. Full-year guidance: revenue ¥99.2 billion (+23.7%), operating profit ¥23.1 billion (+7.7%), net profit ¥16.08 billion (+0.8%). Why: Profit growth was driven by 'BizReach's high margin and high ARPU' and 'rapid revenue expansion at HRMOS.' BizReach alone: revenue ¥38.3 billion, operating margin 42.7% — top-tier among Japanese SaaS / platform companies. HRMOS: ARR ¥8.95 billion (+181%) with quarterly break-even achieved. The 'highly profitable core + rapidly growing new business' combination is exactly what the market loves. Stock reaction: Stock rose over +50% in 2025, reaching ¥8,317 on June 3 (market cap ~¥334.5 billion). Structural drivers behind the stock rise: First, BizReach's overwhelming operating margin (42.7%). BizReach is the leader in Japan's direct-recruiting market (where companies scout candidates directly); its niche-focused, high-ARPU model has secured profitability that exceeds even peers like Recruit and en-japan. Structural labor shortage drives ongoing expansion of the 'high-class job-changing market' — sustained profit growth is expected. Second, HRMOS turning profitable while growing 180%+. SaaS businesses typically endure long loss periods that test investor patience; HRMOS achieved quarterly break-even while sustaining ARR +181%. 'Profitable SaaS × hyper-growth' is globally rare and immediately raised the company's priority within the sector. Third, relative outperformance even during the broader SaaS-stock correction. While loss-making SaaS like freee, Money Forward, Sansan were sold down, Visional earned a premium as 'cash-generating profitable SaaS.' Fourth, record-high earnings outlook + sustained revenue growth. Full-year revenue +23.7% but OP +7.7% looks like deceleration, but this is temporary compression from HRMOS investment being pulled forward — long-term investors are accumulating, expecting larger profit growth after the investment phase ends. Fifth, management evaluation. Founder Soichiro Minami has a long track record in HR and is highly regarded by institutional investors for IR engagement. Risks: (a) recession-driven hiring slowdown shrinking BizReach revenue, (b) HRMOS price competition from major rivals (CyberAgent, Rakus, freee etc.), (c) founder/key-person dependency risk. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY7/2026 H1: Revenue ¥46.61 billion (+26.2%), OP ¥12.77 billion (+24.9%) — HR Tech strong. BizReach leading, HRMOS accelerating. ROA 18.58%, ROE 26.72% — high profitability; equity ratio high. PER 25.6x forecast, PBR 5.65x. Labor demand strength supports further growth expectations. 12 consecutive quarters of strong results, OP/net margins stable, profit base solid.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
All-three-up business with stock up +24.9% YoY — solid steady-grower alignment in 2025. No mismatch to investigate.

4307 Nomura Research Institute,Ltd. SI_IT_services large
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +29.2%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 kabuyoho 4307 + Yahoo finance AI topics 4307 + minkabu 4307 + IFIS 株予報 4307 + Verification on NRI Australia / NA goodwill impairment + ROE 25% mid-term target + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 4307 + minkabu 4307 + Astris MRI report
What happened: Nomura Research Institute (NRI) is one of Japan's largest IT consulting and think-tank firms, focused on building and operating core systems for financial institutions (banks, brokerages, insurers) and policy research/surveys. It's an independent IT company in the Nomura Holdings group. Q3 results were announced on January 29, 2026. Revenue continues to grow YoY, but the trailing 12 quarters show slightly deteriorating performance — net margin compressed YoY, operating margin softening, and ROE/ROA running slightly below conventional thresholds. Why: Continued top-line growth is supported by demand from financial institutions for DX (digitalization), new AI-related projects, and expanding system maintenance/operations contracts. The margin softness is largely from rising engineer-talent costs and upfront investment into new areas. Stock reaction: The stock rose from ¥4,564 (May 26 close) to ¥5,224 (Jun 1 close) — about a 14% gain — and volume on May 29 was +115% vs the prior day. A US major brokerage has a Bullish rating with a price target of ¥5,500 — strong analyst endorsement. Several structural reasons explain why business and stock are rising together. First, strong institutional backing. NRI's shareholder mix is 42.0% institutional, 27.0% insiders (mainly Nomura Holdings, etc.), and 31.1% general shareholders, with 352 institutional investors holding the stock. That stable institutional base provides a price floor. Second, high capital efficiency. Actual ROE is 22.50% — very high even among Japanese companies. Forward PER of 25.6x is elevated, but high ROE justifies it. Third, AI-theme tailwind. NRI launches generative-AI-powered new services and AI solutions for financial institutions, gaining attention as 'Japan's flagship IT name' riding the AI boom. Fourth, structural expansion of financial-institution DX demand. Japan's banks, insurers, and brokerages are simultaneously updating core systems and migrating to the cloud, creating a long-term growth base for NRI's flagship business. Fifth, defensive characteristics. NRI's revenue mix is centered on long-term contracts with large enterprises, so it's relatively immune to economic cycles — making it easy to hold even in risk-off market environments. Profit-growth momentum is dulling, but the market is rewarding NRI's structural positioning and high ROE as 'a company that will keep earning into the future.' [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥814.7 billion (+6.5%) but operating profit collapsed to ¥58.27 billion (-56.8%) on Australia/N. America subsidiary goodwill impairment. FY27 guidance: net profit ¥119 billion (+7.8x) — record-high. Growth drivers: financial-sector development/operations + cloud-infrastructure services. Mid-term ROE target 25%, PER 24.58x / forecast ROE 27.43% reflect high market expectations. Recurring revenue and BPO are stable base.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
All-three-up business with stock up +29.2% YoY — solid steady-grower alignment in 2025. No mismatch to investigate.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (acceleration to new ATH — OP +12%, NP +17.7%, mid-term plan announcement):* stock ¥4,444 (Jan 14) – ¥6,476 (Nov 25); year-end close ¥6,021 (+29.2% YoY, new ATH territory above the 2021 peak ¥5,170 — first time since the 2022 derating). **FY3/2025 actual (announced April 24, 2025): revenue ¥764,813M (+3.8% YoY), operating profit ¥134,907M (+12.0% YoY), operating margin 17.6%, net profit ¥93,800M (+17.7% YoY)** per 決算短信 IFRS / xtech.nikkei.com coverage. Annual dividend ¥77 (vs FY3/2024 ¥74, +¥3). **NRI announced new mid-term plan (V2030) at the April 24, 2025 results: targets revenue ¥1T+ and OP margin 20%+ by FY3/2030, implying ~5.4% rev CAGR and ~9% OP CAGR.** Stock-move catalyst: FY3/2025 operational acceleration (OP/NP growth re-accelerated after FY24's moderation) + new mid-term plan establishing 10-year ¥1T trajectory + V2030 vision; broke through 2021 ATH territory. Sign summary: revenue rose +3.8% YoY; operating profit rose +12.0% YoY; net profit rose +17.7% YoY; operating margin 17.6%; annual dividend lifted ¥3 to ¥77; shares rallied to new ATH ¥6,476 (Nov 25) on FY25 acceleration + V2030 plan; business strong, stock strong. 業績 ◎ (OP/NP growth re-accelerating; FY3/2025 short of mid-term plan rev target ¥810B but strong on OP) / 株価 ◎ (close +29.2%, new ATH).

Cautious side: FY3/2025 revenue ¥764.8B was short of mid-term plan target ¥810B (−5.6%); FY3/2026 Q4 (Jan–Mar 2026, announced April 2026 — AFTER the 2025 window) subsequently reported a ¥67.9B net loss and FY3/2026 NP guidance −83.7% YoY per recent kabuyoho coverage — suggests forward growth thesis may be re-pricing in early 2026, though OUTSIDE the 2021–2025 fixture window. Concentration on Nomura Group revenue remains a structural feature. Global expansion (FY3/2022 ¥123.2B → FY3/2025 target ¥150B) is M&A-driven; integration risk on overseas acquisitions.
