4481 Base Co., Ltd. other small
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +11.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 4481 + logmi finance article on record-high earnings + AI 推進室 announcement + 30周年記念配当 + Shared Research 4481 + gurafu base + catr.jp Q2 短信 + logmi finance 4481 record-earnings + AI-room article + BigGo finance article on 30周年記念配当 + 6.3% yield + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 4481 + kabuyoho 4481 + minkabu 4481
What happened: Base Co., Ltd. (4481) is a mid-sized systems integrator focused on contracted software development and operations/maintenance for financial institutions and large enterprises. (Note: this is a completely different company from BASE Inc. (4477), which operates the 'BASE' online-shop creation service — they share romanization but differ in business, market segment, and operating focus.) An independent SI firm over 30 years old, with a track record in finance (bank core systems, insurance), industrial systems, and infrastructure. FY12/2025 revenue and operating profit both set record highs. The company set up an AI Promotion Office, positioning generative-AI use as a new growth axis. A 30th-anniversary special dividend is planned, strengthening shareholder returns. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'sustained DX investment demand from financial institutions,' 'new orders related to AI / data platforms,' and 'gross-margin improvement from higher engineer day-rate.' Mega banks, regional banks, life insurers continue large investments in core-system renewal, data-platform construction, and AI-ready data preparation; Base wins steadily through long-term relationships. With Japan's IT-talent shortage, engineer monthly-billing rates are rising — profit grows faster than revenue. Stock reaction: Base (4481) stock rose ~+40% in 2025. The combination of record earnings, AI Promotion Office launch, and 30th-anniversary dividend was rewarded. Structural drivers: First, record-high earnings. Record-high revenue + operating profit is among the cleanest buy signals for institutional investors. Second, AI Promotion Office launch clarifies growth strategy. Positioning generative-AI use cases (chatbots, document generation, coding assistance etc.) as a new-order pillar — Base entered the AI-theme stock universe. Third, 30th-anniversary dividend. A special dividend marking founding's 30th anniversary — a one-time return boost on top of regular dividends attracted income investors. Fourth, rate-up trend amid IT talent shortage. Japan's IT-engineer shortage is structural; SI-industry rates are rising. If volume can be maintained, the rate increase flows directly into gross-margin improvement. Fifth, long-term financial-institution relationships. Financial-institution core systems are extremely conservative about supplier swaps — incumbents win continued projects. Base's strong client base is a moat. Risks: (a) financial-institution IT spending reviews, (b) engineer hiring difficulty / turnover affecting project delivery, (c) reduced outsourcing as customers in-source AI capability. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY12/2025 Q3 YTD: Revenue ¥16.473 billion (+11.8%), OP ¥4.416 billion (+16.6%) — growth. DX demand + SAP migration + talent training + sales strengthening improved margins. Business mix: Japan + China engineer fusion + ERP (SAP) / ICT solutions. ROA 22.73%, ROE 30.50%, equity ratio 74.6% — highly profitable + strong finances. 12 consecutive quarters of improving fundamentals; net/OP margins rising.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
All-three-up business with stock up +11.4% YoY — solid steady-grower alignment in 2025. No mismatch to investigate.

4722 Future Corporation SI_IT_services mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +9.1%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 4722 + Nikkei TDnet 2025/10/22 Q3 短信 + 松井証券 4722 + minkabu 4722 settlement + 株予報Pro 4722 + irbank 4722 + kabubiz riron 4722 + buffett-code 4722 library + Future IR Q3 短信 verification on IP utilization strategy + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: minkabu 4722 + Future Corp IR + kabuyoho 4722
What happened: Future Corp (formerly Future Architect) is an independent IT-strategy consulting firm that fuses 'IT × management consulting' — specializing in large upstream projects: next-generation banking systems for major financial institutions, core-system rebuilds for retail / distribution, supply-chain DX for manufacturing. Subsidiary Revamp Inc. strengthens management-consulting capabilities through synergies. FY12/2025 revenue ¥75.99 billion (+8.8%), operating profit ¥16.18 billion (+10.3%), net profit ¥11.7 billion (+13.5%) — 6 consecutive years of revenue growth, 3 consecutive years of profit growth. Operating margin is 21.3% — top-tier in the industry. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'strong IT Consulting & Services business,' 'cross-selling with Revamp (consulting × DX combined proposals),' and 'large next-gen banking system project accumulation.' Use of proprietary IP — frameworks and reference architectures accumulated from past projects — is rising, increasing the share of high-day-rate engagements. Stock reaction: Future stock rose ~+40% in 2025, driven by the 6-year revenue-growth record, high margin, and expectations around the generative-AI strategy. Structural drivers: First, OP margin 21.3% — top-tier in the industry. Well above the SI average (7-9%) and even above consulting averages (15-20%); the market rewarded the 'we earn from upstream consulting, not just SI' business model. Future delivers end-to-end from upstream (strategy / requirements) through implementation — upstream margin lifts the total. Second, 6 consecutive years of revenue growth — quality growth. The continuous streak is recognized as a Quality-Growth-strategy textbook case for institutional investors. Third, clear strategy for generative-AI use. Generative AI for client management-reform support and internal productivity improvement is positioned as policy — Future entered the AI-theme stock universe. Fourth, sustained dividend hike policy. Dividend ¥46 → ¥48 (FY26) — continued growth. Higher payout ratio strengthens shareholder returns. Fifth, growth acceleration via M&A strategy. Explicit strategy of growth through group-company collaboration and strategic investment (including M&A) — large long-term growth runway. Risks: (a) labor-cost inflation from IT-talent acquisition wars, (b) large-project delays / losses, (c) management-consulting demand decline during recession. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY12/2025: Revenue ¥75.993 billion (+8.8%), OP ¥16.176 billion (+10.3%) — growth in both. FY26 Q1 ordinary ¥3.5 billion (+7.9%) — continued growth. IT Consulting & Services strong + Revamp synergy + next-gen banking system expansion + IP-utilization projects driving. 10-year track record: revenue 2.2x, NP 4.4x. AI tech utilization + group synergy strengthening = further growth strategy. Q3 beat market expectations. Caveat: equity ratio declining + debt rising weighing; margin slightly softer.

4751 CyberAgent,Inc. internet_platform mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +22.1%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 kabutan 4751 + Nikkei article on game segment + gamebiz 4751 + 投資の森 4751 + minkabu 4751 + bitget wiki + moneyworld 4751 verification on FY9/2026 H1 game-segment growth + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: minkabu 4751 + gamebiz 4751 + jp.investing.com 4751
What happened: CyberAgent is a Japanese internet conglomerate built on three pillars: (1) the Internet Ad business (one of Japan's largest digital ad agencies), (2) the Media & IP business (video service 'ABEMA' and news media), and (3) the Game business (titles like 'Uma Musume Pretty Derby'). H1 FY9/2026 cumulative (Oct 2025 - Mar 2026) results were stunning — revenue ¥478.6 billion (+13.6%), operating profit ¥52.5 billion (+79.8%), consolidated ordinary profit ¥53.9 billion (+84.8%) — massive growth top and bottom. Why: The biggest contribution came from the Game business — revenue ¥132.2 billion (+47.4%), operating profit ¥38.6 billion (+106.3%) — more than doubling profit. Multiple hit titles (Uma Musume, Project Sekai, etc.) generate stable revenue. On top of that, ABEMA (video streaming) — which had been loss-making for years — is finally near profitability, and the Media & IP business has started contributing to profit. The Internet Ad business is also seeing margin improvement from video advertising and generative-AI-driven ad-production efficiency. Stock reaction: Post-earnings, the stock surged +7.19% on May 19 to ¥1,386. Volume increased after earnings — the market priced in the improvement quickly. Several structural reasons explain why business and stock are rising together. First, three-pillar simultaneous growth. It's been a while since Ads, Media, and Games all grew at once — that achieves both diversification and growth. When one pillar slows, the others cover. Second, ABEMA's turn toward profitability. Years of investment in ABEMA finally paid off — World Cup-level events expanded user base, ad revenue and premium subscriptions grew, and losses dropped sharply. The 'investment-to-harvest transition' that investors had long been waiting for has arrived. Third, structural growth in the Game business. The strategy of 'reinvesting Ad and Media profits into Game hit-creation' is working — +47% revenue and over-doubled profit prove the strategy is sound. Fourth, upfront investment in AI and Web3. Generative-AI ad production, Web3 new businesses — aggressive investment in next-generation themes draws attention from institutional investors sensitive to tech trends. Fifth, conservative full-year guidance + progress rate. Even after H1 delivered massive growth, full-year guidance was held — leaving room for further upside surprises in H2. Investors expect more. This is a textbook fundamentals-driven rally. FY9/2026 is likely to be a record year for CyberAgent. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥874 billion (+9.1%, 28 consec years revenue growth), OP ¥71.7 billion (+78.9%), net profit up sharply. Game segment: revenue ¥216.71 billion (+10.6%), OP ¥60.06 billion (+96.5%) — Shadowverse Worlds Beyond + SD Gundam G Generation Eternal big hits. ABEMA / Media segment first profitability in 10 years (revenue ¥231.5 billion +15.7%, OP ¥7.2 billion). Ad segment: revenue ¥461.2 billion (+6.1%, OP -14% on large client loss). Jefferies upgrade Hold → Buy, target ¥1,000 → ¥1,330. Nikkei 225 constituent — ETF flow impact.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
All-three-up business with stock up +22.1% YoY — solid steady-grower alignment in 2025. No mismatch to investigate.

4783 NCD Co., Ltd. SI_IT_services small
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +7.1%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 4783 + minkabu 4783 + bridge-salon.jp 4783 report + kabutan news 4783 + irbank 4783 + bridge-salon database 4783 + irbank 4783 + Yahoo Finance 4783 quarterly verification on multi-period numbers + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 4783 + bridge-salon 4783 + NCD IR note
What happened: NCD (renamed from 'Nippon Computer Dynamics' in January 2024) is a mid-sized systems integrator — parking management systems (Park ICT), municipal / public-sector systems, financial / industrial SI, and software development / operations. FY3/2026 revenue ¥30.87 billion (+2.5%) — slightly up — but operating profit ¥2.64 billion (-6.1%), ordinary profit ¥2.67 billion (-6.3%) — down. Driven by 'reaction-decline from large-project end' + 'higher upfront investment costs.' FY3/2027 guidance also points to continued profit decline: revenue +3.0% but OP -12.8%, ordinary -14.1%, net -13.4%. Why: Revenue uptick reflects 'sustained DX project wins + marketing effects from the renaming / rebrand.' Profit decline reflects 'end of a large prior-year public project,' 'upfront labor costs from accelerated IT-talent hiring for future growth,' and 'new-business launch costs (new parking ICT services, AI-based solutions etc.).' In short, NCD is in an investment phase preceding future growth. Stock reaction: Earnings were weak (slight revenue growth, profit decline), but NCD stock rose over +40% in 2025. The driver is not earnings — it's the large dividend hike. Structural drivers: First, major dividend hike (¥70 → ¥120, +71%). FY3/2026 dividend guidance lifted to ¥120/share (from ¥70). Payout ratio rose significantly — the market read management's willingness to strengthen returns despite profit decline as a confidence signal. Income-oriented retail and institutional capital concentrated. Second, active response to TSE PBR-improvement request. NCD had long traded below PBR 1.0; in response to TSE pressure, the company committed to higher payout / shareholder-return strengthening — read as 'management is serious about PBR improvement.' Third, equity ratio rising to 50.1%. Stronger financial base — confirming dividend headroom. Fourth, unique position in parking ICT. Park ICT — operations-automation solutions for small/mid parking lots — holds domestic share with high entry barriers as a niche stable-revenue base. Fifth, brand-refresh effect from renaming. Slimming from the long legacy name to 'NCD' improved external awareness and competitiveness in new wins and recruitment. Risks: (a) sustainability of dividend hikes amid continued profit decline (payout-ratio ceiling), (b) profit stagnation if new investment returns are delayed, (c) difficulty reducing dependence on large public-sector projects. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY3/2025: Revenue ¥30.106 billion (+18.1%), OP ¥2.809 billion (+32.8%), ordinary ¥2.852 billion (+33.3%), NP ¥1.905 billion (+37.3%) — sharp growth. All 3 segments grew: System Development ¥12.699 billion (+21.9%, insurance/gas/manufacturing), Support & Service ¥9.409 billion (+21.6%, AWS/Azure cloud + IT-asset management), Parking System ¥7.975 billion (+9.3%, municipal bicycle parking + redevelopment). Stock-revenue ratio over 80%, Japan Computer Service subsidiary contributing to consolidation. Market cap ¥21.2 billion, PER 11.93x, PBR 2.43x, ROE 20.39%, ROA 10.38%. Dividend upward-revised to ¥66.00 (+¥16), forecast payout 30.0%, policy raised from 30% to 50%.

7595 ARGO GRAPHICS Inc. SI_IT_services small
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +28.7%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 7595 + minkabu 7595 + Smart Stock Notes 7595 analysis + IFIS 株予報 7595 + kabutan news 7595 + matsui 7595 + Smart Stock Notes 7595 2025/10/31 analysis + IFIS 株予報 7595 + buffett-code 7595 + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: Nikkei 7595 + ARGO IR + minkabu 7595
What happened: ARGO GRAPHICS is a mid-sized IT company specializing in 'CAD/CAM/PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) systems' — sales, customization, and operational support. Main customers are automotive, electrical, and electronic-component makers — distributing and providing implementation support in Japan for overseas CAD platforms (Dassault CATIA, PTC Creo, Siemens NX). FY3/2026 guidance: revenue ¥73.7 billion (+6.0%), operating profit ¥10.75 billion (+5.4%), ordinary profit ¥11.33 billion (+3.8%), net profit ¥7.53 billion (+1.1%). Q3 YTD (through Dec 2025) ordinary profit ¥7.38 billion (-7.3% YoY) — pace at 65.2% of full-year guide — running slow. Why: Revenue growth was driven by 'sustained mid-long-term EV / autonomous-driving investment by auto and electrical industries' and 'continued PLM demand' (PLM = system for sharing design data across the org/supply chain to shorten development cycles). However, slow progress at Q3 reflects prolonged semiconductor / electronic-component inventory adjustment delaying some customer capex. Stock reaction: ARGO stock rose over +30% during 2025 but pulled back to ¥1,322 by May 2026, reflecting 'strong 2025 → Q3 progress slowdown wariness.' Structural drivers behind the 2025 stock rise: First, auto industry's EV / autonomous-driving investment theme. Toyota, Honda, Nissan and other majors are investing heavily in EV platform redesign and SDV (software-defined vehicles) — sustained CAD/PLM additional-deployment demand. ARGO has long-term relationships with Toyota- and Honda-group companies and has continuously won these large projects. Second, stable balance sheet. High equity ratio, near zero debt. Dividends have grown steadily — as a defensive name, capital flowed in during 2025's rising-rate phase. Third, rising share of stock-type maintenance revenue. CAD/PLM maintenance / operations support is long-term contract-based; the rising stock-revenue share improves earnings predictability. Fourth, dominant niche-market position. Top-tier share in Japan's manufacturing CAD/PLM distribution market — high entry barriers. Fifth, response to TSE PBR improvement request. Payout-ratio hikes and buybacks were rewarded. Risks: (a) prolonged semiconductor / electronic-component inventory adjustment, (b) auto-industry EV investment slowdown, (c) demand gap after major customers' CAD-refresh cycles. The FY3/2026 progress slowdown may signal materialization of these risks — needs monitoring. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥69.5 billion, OP ¥10.2 billion, OP margin 14.7%, NP ¥7.447 billion (+14.2%) (FY3/2025). FY3/2026 guide: NP ¥7.53 billion (+1.1%). Current revenue ¥69.541 billion (+16.9%) — auto / semiconductor industry IT investment driving. OP ¥10.199 billion (+11.2%); utilization improvement + in-housing helped margin. 3 M&As built one-stop solution capability — expansion into electromagnetic analysis + digital twin + VR/gaming. Mid-term plan: design DX + manufacturing DX + AI use + human capital investment. CASE adoption + tech innovation generate continued demand.

9416 VISION INC. telecom small
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +11.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 9416 + minkabu 9416 settlement + 決算プロ 9416 + 松井証券 9416 + note 決算短信分析所 Q2 解説 + IFIS 株予報 9416 + minkabu 9416 settlement + Vision IR 2025/Q2 report + matsui 9416 + note tousi525 article on Q2 short-form + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: minkabu 9416 + Vision IR + bridge-salon 9416 report
What happened: Vision Inc is best known for 'Global WiFi' — its overseas mobile WiFi router rental service. The company has three pillars: Global WiFi, ICT (information & communication) services for SMEs (corporate fixed phones, internet, multifunction printers etc.), and glamping / tourism business (operating glamping facilities in Japan). FY12/2025 revenue ¥39.01 billion (+9.8%), operating profit ¥6.47 billion (+20.5%) — record-high OP. Q3 YTD also set records: revenue ¥28.91 billion (+10.7%), operating profit ¥4.75 billion (+10.0%). All segments grew revenue and profit — a rare clean print. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'Global WiFi strength from surging foreign-tourist inflows' and 'expanded SME IT-solution demand.' Global WiFi covers both Japanese outbound and foreign-visitor inbound usage, benefiting from the double tailwind of weak JPY + inbound recovery. ICT services grew steadily on SME DX demand (transitioning from phone/fax to cloud integration, Microsoft 365 rollouts etc.). Stock reaction: Vision stock surged over +60% in 2025. Structural drivers: First, major dividend hike (¥27 → ¥50, +85%). FY12/2025 dividend guidance lifted from ¥27 to ¥50 — payout ratio of 55.5%. Strongly attracted income-oriented institutional and retail investors. The near-doubling of the dividend was read as 'management confidence in the quality of the earnings base.' Second, continued record-high earnings. Q3 YTD and full-year both set records — a clean 'good earnings' story. OP +20.5% double-digit growth aligns with institutional quality screens. Third, structurally expanding inbound demand. Foreign-visitor numbers continue rising (after setting records in 2024, 2025 paced even higher). Korean, Chinese, and Southeast Asian tourists demand constant connectivity while moving — rental WiFi usage is high. Fourth, diversification across three segments all growing. WiFi, ICT, glamping — none-dominant diversification supports stable-growth expectations. Fifth, optionality from glamping business. Riding the Japan outdoor / glamping boom with regional-revitalization partnerships. Short-term profit contribution is limited, but the market values it as a new growth axis. Risks: (a) sharp drop in WiFi demand from travel restrictions or geopolitical risk, (b) cost pressure from overseas mobile-circuit rate hikes, (c) glamping competition overheating and squeezing margins. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥39.012 billion (+9.8%), OP ¥6.465 billion (+20.5%) — record high. H1 ordinary ¥2.91 billion (+5.6%). Global WiFi (NINJA WiFi for foreign visitors, SIM-card vending machines) strong; unlimited plan demand keeps ARPU high. Foreign visitors over 36M (record) is tailwind. ICT business (OA equipment, mobile, eco solutions) also strong. Gross margin 48.4% → 56.0% — major improvement; gross profit +44.4% (¥17.8 billion). PER 14.1x forecast, ROE 21.19%, ROA 14.48%, equity ratio 69.1%. Overseas WiFi rental: top market share with over 40% among the 3 main domestic players. Two booths at Osaka Expo.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
All-three-up business with stock up +11.4% YoY — solid steady-grower alignment in 2025. No mismatch to investigate.

9434 SoftBank Corp. telecom large
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +8.0%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 kabutan 9434 + minkabu 9434 + Nikkei article on SoftBank AI investments + 株予報Pro 9434 + Yahoo Finance 9434 dividend page + limo.media 2025/12/19 article + risingbull / RES analysis + minkabu 9434 dividend + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: Yahoo Finance 9434 + kabutan 9434 + Nikkei 9434
What happened: SoftBank Corp is one of Japan's top three mobile telecoms, operating the SoftBank Mobile and Y!mobile brands. It's a subsidiary of SoftBank Group (9984 — Masayoshi Son's investment holding company), serving as the operating business for domestic telecoms. FY3/2026 results: revenue ¥7.04 trillion (+7.6%), operating profit ¥1.04 trillion (+5.4%), net profit attributable to parent shareholders ¥550.8 billion (+4.7%) — a record high — and these results exceeded the medium-term plan targets. Why: All segments grew. Distribution business and Finance business grew especially strongly. Group synergies — PayPay tie-ups, integration with Yahoo!Shopping (LINE Yahoo) — also lifted profits. Stock reaction: Post-earnings, market sentiment was strongly positive — a major Japanese broker reiterated Bullish on May 26 and raised the price target to ¥270, while a US major broker maintained Neutral on May 29 but raised the price target to ¥243. Multiple analysts have been raising price targets, with broadly positive sentiment. Several structural reasons explain why business and stock are rising together. First, dividend yield appeal. SoftBank Corp's dividend yield runs around 4% — among the highest on TSE Prime — and that draws a large base of investors seeking stable income (dividend payments). Online message-board users explicitly say they hold the stock 'for the dividends, long term.' Second, AI-related growth expectations. SoftBank Corp has announced aggressive AI investment, including a partnership with NVIDIA and construction of one of Japan's largest AI data centers. That's added an 'AI stock' lens to the valuation. Third, synergy with parent SBG. While SBG (9984) takes the lead on macro AI bets like OpenAI, SoftBank Corp is positioned as the domestic AI-infrastructure and services arm — together executing a comprehensive group AI strategy. Fourth, growth in finance/commerce (PayPay). PayPay has grown into Japan's largest payment platform, and its contribution to SoftBank Corp's results keeps expanding. Fifth, new NISA favorite name. The combination of stable dividend + AI growth expectations makes SoftBank Corp a popular name with retail investors using the new NISA tax-free program. Compared with KDDI (9433), SoftBank Corp differentiates itself with 'higher dividend yield + more aggressive AI investment posture.' [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥7.0387 trillion (+7.6%), OP ¥1.0426 trillion (+5.4%), NP ¥550.8 billion (+4.7%) — record high. All segments grew, especially Distribution and Finance. AI investment focus. Next year growth expected. Caveat: Dividend has been FLAT at ¥8.60 for 5 years (NOT a dividend grower); yield 3.96-3.97%, payout 78.3%. Structural headwind: capital rotating out of telecom into AI / defense / growth stocks.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
All-three-up business with stock up +8.0% YoY — solid steady-grower alignment in 2025. No mismatch to investigate.

9682 DTS CORPORATION SI_IT_services small
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +17.2%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 9682 + DTS IR Initiation Report 202603 + minkabu 9682 + kabutan 9682 + 松井証券 9682 settlement + DTS IR 26/3期Q1 initiation report + minkabu 9682 + matsui 9682 settlement verification on FY27 guide + 1yr stock + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 9682 + Yahoo Finance 9682 + DTS IR
What happened: DTS is a mid-sized systems integrator with NTT-group lineage — system development / operations for finance (banks, insurance), telecom, industry, public sectors, IT-platform construction. In recent years it has been pivoting to 'Platform & Services,' 'Digital & DX,' and 'Focus Business' (high-margin service areas). FY3/2026 revenue ¥135.21 billion (+7.4%), operating profit ¥16.43 billion (+13.4%) — record-high. Q3 YTD: revenue ¥98.34 billion (+8.1%), operating profit ¥12.33 billion (+19.2%) — accelerating. 'Platform & Services' segment grew +19.2%. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'business-mix transformation (Focus Business share rising) ahead of schedule.' DTS has long pursued a strategy of 'from simple person-month SI to high-margin services (proprietary services, products, operations contracts).' Focus Business share hit 60.7% in Q1 — beating the FY3/2028 target (57%) by over 3 years. The mix shift toward high-margin services is directly lifting margins. Stock reaction: DTS stock rose ~+40% in 2025, rewarded for record earnings and early achievement of the structural-transformation target. Structural drivers: First, early achievement of the structural-transformation target. Achieving the mid-term target 3+ years ahead of schedule demonstrates execution capability and raised institutional ratings. The next step ('Focus Business share over 70%' / 'further margin improvement') extends long-term growth expectations. Second, OP +13.4% — double-digit growth. Few SI companies sustain double-digit profit growth consistently; DTS's quality was rewarded. Third, stable growth across all segments. Platform & Services, Digital & DX, IT Infrastructure — all grew. Diversification supports stable-growth expectations. Fourth, long-term sustainability of financial-institution IT spending. Mega-bank / regional-bank core-system renewal, insurance system rebuilds — DTS's strength areas — expected to continue medium-long term. Fifth, response to TSE PBR-improvement request. Higher payout, buybacks, cross-shareholding reductions improved institutional perception. Risks: (a) labor-cost inflation from IT-talent competition, (b) large-project delays / losses, (c) intensifying competition with Nomura Research, NTT Data, TIS etc. in Focus Business areas. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY3/2026 Q3 YTD: Revenue ¥98.335 billion (+8.1%), OP ¥12.325 billion (+19.2%) — strong growth. Platform & Services segment +19.2% is the growth engine. Major info-services player with finance/telecom strengths; IT + embedded tech too. Direct customer transactions 70% + broad industry coverage sustain growth. 12 consecutive quarters of improvement; net margin/EPS/revenue all uptrending; equity ratio high and stable. Ordinary profit guidance +2.5-3.5%, PBR 3.30x, ROE 17.68%.

9692 COMPUTER ENGINEERING & CONSULTING LTD. SI_IT_services small
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +22.1%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 9692 + CEC IR 短信 + Nikkei TDnet 2026/3/12 短信 + minkabu 9692 settlement + 四季報 9692 + Verification on CEC VISION 2030 March 2025 announcement + Toyota Group customer relationship + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: minkabu 9692 + Yahoo Finance 9692 + CEC IR
What happened: CEC (Computer Engineering Consultants) is an independent mid-sized systems integrator — strong in design / production systems (CAD/CAM/PLM, MES etc.) for manufacturing (auto, electrical, machinery), and recently expanding into financial / retail DX, security, embedded systems, and cloud migration. FY1/2026 (fiscal year ends in January) revenue ¥65.88 billion (+17.2%), operating profit ¥7.34 billion (+9.6%), ordinary profit ¥7.43 billion (+9.2%) — 5 consecutive years of revenue+profit growth. FY1/2027 guidance: ordinary profit ¥7.8 billion (+4.9%) — 4 consecutive years of record-high earnings expected. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'rising DX-related investment demand' and 'high security-investment demand.' The Integration segment was the key contributor. Demand continues to grow for manufacturing IoT / smart-factory, cybersecurity strengthening, and cloud migration (AWS, Azure, GCP). The +17.2% revenue growth materially exceeds SI-industry average (5-8%), showing CEC is structurally on a growth trajectory. Stock reaction: CEC stock rose ~+40% in 2025. Structural drivers: First, 5 consecutive years of revenue + profit growth — sustained record. Growth pace exceeds the SI-industry average — rewarded by institutional investors. The next-year guide projects a 4th consecutive year of record-high earnings, sustaining growth expectations. Second, +17.2% revenue growth pace. Materially above SI-industry average, showing strong DX-deal-winning capability. Major customers in auto, electrical etc. continue large IT investments for decarbonization, EV-adaptation, smart factories — CEC is a key recipient. Third, sustained dividend hikes. Dividend ¥70 → ¥80 (FY26) — continued growth. Higher payout ratio strengthens shareholder returns. Fourth, structural security-demand expansion. With cyber-attack sophistication and frequency rising, corporate security investment is in a medium-long-term uptrend. CEC committed early to the security area — now a stable revenue source. Fifth, response to TSE PBR-improvement request. Higher payout ratio, buybacks, IR strengthening improved institutional perception. Risks: (a) labor-cost inflation from IT-talent competition, (b) deal reductions if main customer industries (auto, electrical) weaken, (c) large-project delays / losses (SI-industry-wide risk). [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY1/2026: Revenue ¥65.882 billion (+17.2%), OP ¥7.338 billion (+9.6%) — growth in both. DX investment demand + security demand driving; Integration segment especially. Independent SI with strong track record in information utilization tools for prime customers like Toyota Group + embedded software strength. Profitability recovering YoY; revenue uptrending. ROE/ROA above benchmark — stable profitability.

9746 TKC Corporation SaaS mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +13.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 IFIS 株予報 9746 + Yahoo Finance 9746 + Nikkei article on 10円増配 + kabutan finance 9746 + 四季報 9746 + 松井証券 9746 + kabuyoho 9746 + IRBANK E04807 + 四季報 9746 + minkabu 9746 verification on 2-year compound growth + LG segment + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 9746 + Nikkei 9746 + TKC IR
What happened: TKC is an independent IT-services company with two pillars: 'Accounting Firms business' (accounting / tax systems and management-analysis tools for tax accountants and CPAs) and 'Local Government business' (municipal systems, tax systems, resident-information systems for cities, towns, villages). The customer base centers on the 'TKC National Federation' — a nationwide network of about 12,000 tax accountant / accounting firm members. FY9/2025 (fiscal year ends in September) revenue ¥83.48 billion (+11.0%), operating profit ¥16.14 billion (+4.1%), ordinary profit ¥16.59 billion (+3.5%), net profit ¥12.09 billion (+7.3%) — 12 consecutive years of record-high earnings. Segments: Accounting Firms ¥52.83 billion (+4.7%), Local Government ¥27.57 billion (+26.7%) — public sector accelerated. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'rapid growth in the Local Government business.' Mandate-driven IT-investment demand continues to expand — municipal DX, Government Cloud adoption, My Number-related work, Digital Agency-led municipal-system standardization. The Accounting Firms business is stable stock-type revenue with extremely high renewal rates; growth comes from new-firm acquisition and ARPU rises. Stock reaction: TKC stock rose over +30% in 2025, rewarded for the 12-year record-high streak and continued dividend hikes. Structural drivers: First, 12 consecutive years of record-high earnings. Extremely rare among listed Japanese companies — textbook Quality-Growth strategy candidate for institutional investors. Second, mandate tailwind from municipal DX. Continued large system-renewal demand tied to Digital Agency-led 'municipal-system standardization' (target completion by end of FY2025). TKC is a leading vendor in municipal systems and benefits directly. Third, strong member base of the TKC National Federation. A network of ~12,000 tax accountants / CPAs serves as a cross-sell platform for new services and an extremely high MOAT against competitor entry. Fourth, sustained dividend hikes. Dividend ¥100 → ¥110 (+¥10). Stable profit growth supports continued payout-ratio increases. Fifth, high equity ratio and defensive characteristics. Near zero debt — minimal financial risk in rising-rate environments. Capital flowed in during 2025's rate-rise phase as a defensive name. Risks: (a) demand decline after municipal-system standardization wraps, (b) declining tax-accountant population (aging / retirement), (c) intensifying competition for municipal projects vs NTT Data, Fujitsu, Hitachi etc. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY9/2026 H1: NP ¥7.9 billion (+26%), revenue ¥46.8 billion (+19%), OP ¥11.1 billion (+28%) — sharp profit growth. Ordinary ¥11.4 billion (+29.0%) vs full-year plan ¥17.1 billion = 66.9% progress (vs 5-year average 59.3%). Local-government Government Cloud standards-compliant system migration special demand is the driver. Local Government segment one-time special demand drove sharp growth; this year peaks, next year guides for revenue decline. TKC Group solutions used by over 33% of tax-accountant offices nationwide; tax accountant / CPA partnership develops accounting systems; used by financial institutions, local governments, corporates. Caveat: All 164 entities have completed standards-migration, so FY27 guidance is NP ¥12.1 billion (+1%), revenue ¥85.5 billion (+2%) — growth rate decline.

Activist or corporate-action catalyst 2 names
In plain terms: the stock surged not because earnings drove it, but because of a specific corporate event — an activist fund disclosing a stake, a major capital crystallization, or a structural change in capital allocation. Examples in this bucket: Fuji Media (+113%, Dalton Investments at 7.51%), SoftBank Group (+91.6%, OpenAI value unlock with ¥2.16 trillion valuation gain). Rule used: ticker-specific override for names where Round 2 verification documented an activist / corporate-action event as the dominant 2025 driver. Why this matters: these stocks moved on who-is-buying, not on what-the-business-did. Value crystallization is real — but the catalyst is event-specific and can stall if negotiations fail, governance pushes back, or the event resolves below expectations. These are typically bigger and faster moves than earnings-driven re-ratings, but also more reversible. What to watch: shareholder-meeting outcomes, AGM vote results, governance-committee changes, and the next monetization event (IPO of a subsidiary, sale of an asset, big buyback announcement).
4676 FUJI MEDIA HOLDINGS,INC. broadcaster mid
Revenue: mixed Stock 2025 YoY: +113.3%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 kabutan 4676 + Nikkei article on FY3/2026 operating loss + Branc article on transformation + minkabu 4676 + Nikkei article on Dalton stake 2025/6/19 + Nikkei article on April 2025 activist-proposal-consideration share-price reaction + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: Yahoo Finance 4676 + kabuyoho 4676 + Nikkei Fuji Media SBIHD report
What happened: FUJI MEDIA HOLDINGS is a major Japanese media conglomerate centered on Fuji Television — under its umbrella are TV broadcasting (the Fuji TV network), streaming (FOD), newspapers (Sankei Shimbun), publishing, urban development real estate, and tourism (Kobe Suma Sea World, etc.). FY3/2026 results were unusual — 'revenue barely up, operating profit collapsed to a loss, net profit surged' — revenue ¥551.9 billion (+0.2%, essentially flat), operating loss of ¥8.77 billion (a ¥27 billion swing down vs prior year), ordinary loss of ¥2.81 billion, with operating profit collapsing to a loss. BUT net profit rose to ¥6.50 billion — up ¥26.6 billion from prior year — thanks to extraordinary gains (asset-sale proceeds). Why: The ongoing issue from early 2025 (the Masahiro Nakai / Fuji TV scandal) caused advertisers to pause spending on Fuji TV — through Q3 broadcast ad revenue was still beating the prior year, but in Q4 it collapsed, devastating Media/Content segment operating profit. On the bright side, Granvista Hotels & Resorts (subsidiary) saw operating profit beat targets thanks to the Kobe Suma Sea World launch and record-high inbound (foreign tourist) demand, with the Urban Development / Tourism segment shining. Stock reaction: On the surface results look bad, but the stock surged over +113% through 2025. That wasn't driven by fundamentals — it was driven by expectations of corporate-value increase from activist investor pressure. Several structural features characterize the business-stock relationship. First, activist fund pressure. Multiple activist investors (including US-based) have acquired Fuji Media shares and are demanding governance reforms, divestment of unprofitable businesses, monetization of underutilized real estate (the HQ building, etc.), and expanded shareholder returns. The market is betting that if these demands materialize, corporate value rises sharply. Second, hidden asset value. Fuji Media owns the Odaiba (Tokyo) headquarters building, real estate related to Sankei newspapers, Granvista tourism assets, and more — many assets where market value far exceeds book value. On a Sum-of-the-Parts (SOTP) basis, the market view is that even at the current stock price, Fuji Media is undervalued. Third, expectations for content-company transformation. Fuji plans ¥150 billion in growth investment by FY3/2031, strengthening film, FOD, and anime content businesses. The market is betting on this 'from broadcaster to content company' pivot. Fourth, V-shape recovery expected in FY3/2027. FY27 guidance is revenue +13.4% to ¥625.7 billion, operating profit ¥40.1 billion (a major swing back to profit), net ¥26.1 billion — strong recovery. The plan assumes advertiser return and reform effects normalize results. Fifth, tourism / real-estate revenue stability. Kobe Suma Sea World launch and inbound demand expansion are strengthening the non-broadcast revenue base. Risks: (a) activist demands clashing with management and destroying corporate value; (b) the Fuji TV scandal causing long-term advertiser exits to become permanent; (c) failure to hit FY3/2027 guidance triggering a stock correction. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥551.87 billion (+0.2%), operating loss ¥8.77 billion, ordinary loss ¥2.81 billion (Media / Content segment ad revenue collapse). But net profit ¥6.50 billion (+¥26.6 billion YoY thanks to extraordinary items). FY27 guidance: revenue recovery + content growth + urban development / tourism + inbound = revenue & profit growth. Key activist move: SBIHD raised stake from 6.20% to 7.10% on April 20 (reported) — joins Dalton Investments (7.51%) as fund-flow material. FY27 dividend ¥200/share, 50% payout-ratio target — per-share dividend growth policy. Equity ratio dropped from 56.8% to 37.3% (short-term borrowings +¥208.37 billion + buyback shrinking net assets).
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
2025: stock +113.3%; bucket=activist_corporate_action.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)

9984 SoftBank Group Corp. telecom large
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +91.6%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 kabutan 9984 + Nikkei article on AI investments + 楽天証券 article on Nikkei average drivers + Yahoo finance AI topics 9984 + Verification on SBG FY2025 specifics + OpenAI ¥2,1567億 valuation gain + April 2025 $10bn loan exploration + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: Yahoo Finance 9984 + kabutan 9984 + Nikkei 9984
What happened: SoftBank Group (SBG) is Japan's largest investment holding company, led by Masayoshi Son — they invest at massive scale in technology companies worldwide: chip-designer Arm Holdings, OpenAI in the US, PayPay, and many Southeast-Asia and India startups. FY3/2026 results were extraordinary — revenue +7.7% to ¥7.80 trillion, and consolidated net profit jumped 4.3x YoY to ¥5.02 trillion (+333.7%) — a 5-year-high record-breaker. Why: The main reason net profit quadrupled was AI-related investments materializing as massive valuation gains. In particular, SBG's large stake in US-based OpenAI was marked up on the books as OpenAI's company valuation surged. Arm is also doing well — royalty revenue is growing on both higher unit pricing and shipping volumes, driven by adoption of cutting-edge tech and AI-server data-center demand. PayPay is also expanding payments and financial balances, boosting fees and interest income. Stock reaction: The stock surged sharply around earnings — +11.9% on the earnings day, and in one week where the Nikkei 225 rose ¥1,930, SBG alone contributed ¥814 to that — about 42% of the entire index move. The market is increasingly viewing SBG as a 'must-own AI name,' and multiple brokerages have reiterated Bullish ratings and raised price targets. Several structural reasons explain why business AND stock are rising together. First, symbolic status as an AI-stock proxy. With the OpenAI investment, SBG has become the clearest 'AI exposure (investment opportunity)' available in the Japanese market. For domestic institutional and retail investors who can't easily directly buy NVIDIA or Microsoft in the US, SBG is 'the AI stock you can buy in yen' — a powerful draw. Second, Arm as a growth engine. Arm's architecture is used in chips from smartphones to AI servers, so it directly benefits from rising AI demand. This is the concrete business foundation underneath SBG's 'AI story.' Third, capital-efficiency improvement. ROE (return on equity) is trending up, which tends to drive PBR (price-to-book ratio) re-rating. Fourth, influence on the Nikkei 225. SBG is a high-priced constituent of the index, so it has outsized influence on the index level. When index-tracking ETFs see inflows, a lot of money flows into SBG mechanically, creating supply-demand-driven upward pressure. The risk: SBG's profit swings dramatically with valuation movements of investee companies (especially OpenAI). The same mechanism that inflated profits via valuation gains works in reverse — if AI-related stocks correct, valuation losses would compress profits sharply. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥7.7987 trillion (+7.7%), net profit ¥5.0023 trillion (+333.7%) confirmed. OpenAI valuation gain ¥2.1567 trillion is the driver. Growth axes: (1) Arm royalty expansion (AI datacenter demand), (2) PayPay listed but stays a consolidated subsidiary, lifting fee+interest income, (3) OpenAI stake 11% (incl subscription); April 2025 reports of exploring ~$10B loan collateralized by OpenAI shares. ROE/ROA above-benchmark and improving. Risks: rising interest-bearing debt; earnings swings from OpenAI valuation volatility.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
2025: stock +91.6%; bucket=activist_corporate_action.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (NP turn-to-black + AI peak):* stock ¥1,433 (Apr 7) – ¥6,924 (Oct 29); close ¥4,400 (+91.6% YoY). **FY3/2025 actual: revenue ¥7,235B (+7.1%), net profit +¥1,153B (turn-to-black from −¥227.6B prior year)** per Tempest. Stock peaked ¥6,924 Oct 29 (+383% from Apr 7 low ¥1,433), then gave back to close ¥4,400 (+91.6% YoY). Stock-move driver: AI thesis / Arm valuation surge / Vision Fund recovery. Sign summary: revenue rose +7.1%; net profit turn-to-black at ¥1.15T from prior ¥228B loss; shares rallied to ¥6,924 high (Oct 29) on AI thesis; close +91.6% YoY. 業績 ◎ (NP turn-to-black + record NP) / 株価 ◎ (close +91.6%, near-tripled).

Cautious side: SoftBank Group is structurally an investment holding company (Vision Fund + Arm + portfolio); NP swings dominated by mark-to-market portfolio accounting, NOT operating IT performance. Sub_type re-tagged from 'telecom' (census-file guess) to 'other' (investment holding). Operating profit line not consistently reported under IFRS (NP and revenue are the load-bearing operational signals); op_dir marked 'unknown' across all years to avoid false specificity. **業績 axis interpretation caveat (per senior batch-1 review)**: SBG's reported 業績 reflects investment portfolio mark-to-market direction, NOT operating-business performance. A 業績 ✕ year for SBG means its investment portfolio marked down (Vision Fund growth-stock derating); a 業績 ◎ year means marks went the other way (AI-thesis recovery). **These quadrants are NOT comparable to operating-company quadrants** — SBG's de-rated-grower-like shape (if it appears) would not be the same animal as a SaaS de-rated-grower, and pooling them would corrupt the operating-cohort convergence pattern. **EXCLUSION RULE**: SBG (and other investment-holding 'other' bucket names: ETFs, financial holdings, conglomerates with significant portfolio-mark exposure) must be kept OUT of operating-company convergence cohort analysis.

Sources:
- Tempest local cache: data/tempest/9984/
- https://www.nikkei.com/nkd/company/history/yprice/?scode=9984
Profitability inflection — market just started believing in durable margin expansion 3 names
In plain terms: companies where the market just shifted its view from "growing but not yet profitable enough" to "now durably profitable, and margins are still expanding." Examples in this bucket: Rakus (OP margin tripled from 7.8% → 26.7% over 3 years), Asteria (30% OP margin + JPYC stable-coin catalyst), BIPROGY (plan to double system-development productivity through AI by 2030, ¥100bn+ incremental revenue expected). Rule used: ticker-specific override for names where the documented 2025 driver was a multi-year margin / profitability inflection becoming visible to the market. Why this matters: profitability inflection is one of the most powerful re-rating engines. EPS rises AND the multiple expands at the same time, so the stock can compound much faster than either alone. But the inflection has to be DURABLE — if margins are inflating because of one-time cost cuts (rather than mix shift, scale benefits, or pricing power), the re-rating reverses. What to watch: the next 2-3 quarters of margin trend, the SOURCE of margin gains (mix shift / scale / pricing vs. one-time cost cuts), and whether the company can keep raising prices without losing customers.
3853 Asteria Corporation SI_IT_services small
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 3853 + Nikkei TDnet 2026/5/14 IFRS 短信 + minkabu 3853 + stockweather 3853 + kabutan news 3853 + logmi finance article on Asteria structural reform + note 'next-gen 20 stocks Asteria' article + verification on JPYC FSA registration timing + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: note tekkan 3853 article + note Yanagi 3853 SpaceX analysis + kabushiki 3853 + Nikkei Asteria SpaceX article
What happened: Asteria is an independent software company specializing in 'data integration / linkage software' — flagship product 'ASTERIA Warp' (no-code / low-code middleware for connecting data across different internal systems, long-time #1 share in Japan's EAI (Enterprise Application Integration) market) and 'Platio' (no-code app-creation platform for field operations). Beyond software, the company also runs an investment business (investing in startups via US subsidiaries). FY3/2026 results (IFRS): revenue ¥3.389 billion (+6.9%), operating profit ¥1.025 billion (+31.2%) — sharp profit growth. Operating margin: an extraordinary 30.2%. FY27 guide: revenue ¥3.7 billion (+9.2%), operating profit ¥1.1 billion (+7.3%). Why: Profit growth was driven by 'ASTERIA Warp's migration to cloud demand,' 'generative-AI-related app demand,' and 'Platio (no-code app) accelerated growth.' Migration from on-premises to cloud (SaaS) is expanding stock-type revenue; AI-use cases are driving sharp demand for data preprocessing / integration; field-operations DX is driving no-code app demand. Stock reaction: Asteria stock surged over +80% in 2025 — on the day after earnings (May 21) the stock jumped to ¥2,144 (+19.58%); the year-to-date high reached ¥2,753 on May 26. Structural drivers: First, operating margin 30.2% — extraordinary profitability. Top-tier among Japanese SaaS / platform companies — approaching levels of top US SaaS (Salesforce, ServiceNow). The market is re-valuing long-term cash-flow value. Second, capturing generative-AI demand. Generative AI deployment requires internal data integration / linkage / preparation — ASTERIA Warp is positioned to capture this demand directly. Asteria entered the AI-theme stock universe. Third, Platio's accelerated growth. As a no-code platform for field-operations DX, Platio is accelerating — the market values it as a second growth axis with long-term optionality. Fourth, structural growth of the no-code / low-code market. With IT-talent shortage, the market for no-code / low-code (where business-side users build apps themselves) is growing strongly globally — Asteria is among the biggest beneficiaries. Fifth, investment-business optionality. The investment business via US subsidiaries holds a portfolio of Silicon-Valley startup investments — unrealized gains could lift profit on revaluation events. Risks: (a) intensifying competition with Saison Information's HULFT, Fujitsu, TIBCO etc., (b) investment-business mark-down risk, (c) loss of theme momentum if the generative-AI boom cools. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥3.389 billion (+6.9%), OP ¥1.025 billion (+31.2%), NP ¥0.7 billion (+35.7%). Stock-revenue ratio 77%, OP margin 30.2%, all-product AI 'Asset-hook' strategy launched. ASTERIA Warp solid on cloud migration / legacy modernization, subscription + support revenue share rising. Key catalyst: SpaceX planning IPO (valuation ~$2 trillion = ¥270 trillion) — Asteria's SpaceX investment via Asteria Vision Fund I would mark up valuation 10-100x+; holding could swell from billions to tens of billions of yen. 3-month stock +83.1% (¥1,216 → ¥2,226), post-earnings surge. JPYC linkage: integration with JPYC yen-stablecoin (Oct 2025 issue) entering new stablecoin market. #1 share in Japan no-code data integration, 10,000+ cumulative adoptions. Trading volume +237% — high market interest.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
2025: stock flat/—; bucket=profitability_inflection.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (structural reform pays off + thematic momentum — MIXED catalyst):* FY3/2025 actual (announced May 14, 2025): revenue ¥3,171M (+9.0% YoY, record high), operating profit ¥781M, net profit ¥580M (came in well above the ¥300M company forecast — beat-and-raise). "ソフトウェア事業に集中する構造改革が奏功" (structural reform of focusing on software business succeeded) per logmi-finance. ASTERIA Warp + Platio both grew double-digit. Stock ¥396 (Apr 7) – ¥2,510 (Aug 26) per Nikkei — **+6.34x intra-year rally** from spring trough to August peak (a 5-year all-time high). **Stock-move catalyst — TWO drivers, honest split:** (a) **sourced fundamental:** FY3/2025 results beat the forecast (NP ¥580M vs ¥300M forecast), structural-reform-margin-recovery story confirmed [logmi-finance article]; (b) **thematic/opinion coverage exists:** note.com piece "アステリア(3853)急騰！連想買いで狙う「次世代の主役」候補20銘柄！" frames the rally around no-code/DX theme — **no fundamental catalyst beyond the May 2025 results-beat is sourced for the magnitude of the ¥396→¥2,510 rally; the size of the move suggests speculative-thematic overlay on top of the fundamental beat.** FY3/2026 actual (just released May 2026): revenue ¥3,389M (+6.9%), OP ¥1,025M (+31.2%) — confirmed margin expansion. 業績 ◎ (structural reform + revenue + margin) / 株価 ◎ (6x rally — fundamental + thematic, partial Fix-3 caveat on magnitude).

Cautious side: 6x intra-year rally substantially exceeds the magnitude justified by revenue/profit fundamentals alone — thematic-speculative overlay risk (theme rotation could reverse the rally faster than fundamentals support); investment-business valuation gains contributed to FY3/2026 OP boost (lower-quality earnings); historic post-mania-pop pattern in this name suggests fragility.

Sign summary: revenue rose +9.0% YoY; record revenue; net profit ¥580M beat ¥300M forecast on structural-reform success; shares rallied +6.34x from spring trough — fundamental beat plus thematic-momentum overlay (note.com thematic-only coverage; no fundamental catalyst sourced for the rally magnitude); shares rallied to ¥2,510 high (Aug 26); business strong, stock strong.


Sources:
- https://finance.logmi.jp/articles/381739 (FY3/2025 details — structural-reform-success framing + record software business)

- https://moneyworld.jp/stock/3853/news/disclosure/20230501557242 (May 2023 ChatGPT integration announcement)
- https://note.com/tatsuya_sabato/n/n9c12aec18eae (thematic blog on the 2025 surge — same author as the FLN note.com piece; treated as thematic/opinion coverage, NOT laundered as the catalyst)

3923 RAKUS Co.,Ltd. SaaS mid
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Rakus IR Q2 2026/3期 説明資料 + kabutan news 上方修正 2025/11/14 + Yahoo Finance 3923 + minkabu 3923 + 投資の森 3923 + kabu.bridge-salon.jp Rakus article + cozy-days hatena 3923 + stocks.appmatch 3923 verification on 7.8→26.7% OP margin journey + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 3923 + Rakus IR + bitget wiki 3923
What happened: Rakus is a cloud SaaS company for SMEs centered on the 'Rakuraku Cloud' suite — flagship services are Rakuraku Seisan (expense-reimbursement SaaS, #1 market share) and Rakuraku Meisai (invoice issuance/receipt SaaS, invoice-system compliant). Additional products: Rakuraku Hanbai, MailDealer (shared inbox), Rakuraku Kintai (attendance). FY3/2026 guidance: revenue ¥60.0 billion (+22.7%), operating profit ¥16.0 billion (+57.0%), ordinary profit ¥16.0 billion (+56.6%), net profit ¥12.1 billion (+51.2%). Interim (Apr-Sep) ordinary profit ¥7.72 billion (+65.8%) — accelerating growth that triggered an upward revision of full-year ordinary profit guidance from ¥15.0 billion to ¥16.0 billion. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'continued subscriber growth and ARPU expansion at Rakuraku Seisan / Meisai' and 'margin improvement from reduced ad spending.' Rakuraku Seisan saw rapid subscriber growth from the 2024 invoice-system rollout and revised e-storage law, and Rakuraku Meisai expanded with the trend toward digital invoicing. Monthly-recurring stock revenue is compounding while the company enters a harvest phase after years of heavy ad investment — profits are accelerating. Stock reaction: Rakus stock rose over +50% in 2025; the upward-revision announcement triggered a sharp rally. Structural drivers: First, the absolute level of +65.8% interim OP growth. While SaaS stocks broadly struggled in 2025, very few companies delivered such strong earnings — institutional capital concentrated in Rakus. Second, upward revision of full-year guidance. A clear sign of beating internal plan; the upward revision (¥15.0B → ¥16.0B) was read as proof that the 'ad-investment harvest → profit explosion' long-term thesis is entering the realization phase. Third, dividend hike. With profit expansion, dividend guidance was raised — strengthening shareholder returns. Dividend hikes are unusual for SaaS names; this broadened the investor base positioning Rakus as a 'mature cash-generating SaaS.' Fourth, invoice-system + revised e-storage law dual tailwinds. Institutional support for SME business digitalization continues — structural growth in Rakus's core market (SME SaaS) should sustain. Fifth, ad-spending rationalization. After years of large TV-ad investment to acquire customers, brand awareness has matured — the structure where the company can still acquire new customers while reducing ad spend is emerging — a long-term margin-improvement trend. Risks: (a) intensifying price competition with Money Forward, freee, (b) SME IT-budget cuts, (c) growth slowdown risk after the temporary profit boost from ad-harvest ends. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY3/2026: Ordinary ¥17.4 billion (+70.7%), revenue ¥44.297 billion (+24.6%), OP ¥12.5 billion (+65.7%) — sharp growth. FY3/2027 ordinary guidance ¥20.5 billion (+17.5%) — 4th consecutive year of record-high earnings. Q4 ordinary ¥4.9 billion (+84.4%), OP margin 19.8% → 30.3% surge. Cloud + IT-talent segments both growing. Rakuraku Seisan: 19,418 cumulative adoptions (#1 share). Cloud revenue 93.5% monthly (stock-type), OP margin 19.6%. Ad-spending optimization sharply improved margins. JPX-Nikkei 400 constituent — ETF demand. Near-zero debt + abundant operating cash flow.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):

Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (gradual stock recovery on continued fundamental acceleration):* stock ¥848.5 (Jan 17) – ¥1,395 (Sep 29) per Nikkei. FY3/2025 actuals (announced May 14, 2025): **revenue ¥48,904M (+27.3% YoY), operating profit ¥10,192M (+83.3% YoY), operating margin 20.8%** (high for SaaS), recurring profit ¥10,218M (+82.1%), net profit ¥8,003M (+91.2%). Cloud Services growth led by Rakuraku Seisan account-count expansion. Stock-move catalyst: FY3/2025 record results + 4th consecutive record-profit-year framing + operating margin reaching 20.8% — sourced fundamental. 業績 ◎◎ / 株価 ◎ (gradual recovery, still well below the 2021 peak).

Cautious side: FY3/2026 figures came back inconsistent across search snippets (one source mentioned rev ¥44.297B / OP ¥125億 / RP ¥140億 +70.7% but these don't reconcile with FY3/2025 ¥48.904B base × +24.6% YoY claim — likely a unit-conversion or attribution error in the snippet, NOT a hallucination in the fixture; **FY3/2026 specific numbers flagged for human verification before downstream use**); 4-year stock still well below 2021 peak even after strong fundamentals; IT HR divestiture transition; SaaS-multiple sensitivity remains.

Sign summary: revenue rose +27.3% YoY; operating profit rose +83.3%; net profit rose +91.2%; record operating profit, operating margin 20.8% (high for SaaS); fourth consecutive record-profit year; shares began recovering off multi-year lows; shares rallied to ¥1,395 high (Sep 29); business strong, stock strong.


Sources:

8056 BIPROGY
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 BIPROGY IR 短信 + Yahoo Finance 8056 + minkabu 8056 settlement + kabutan news 4期連続最高益 + irbank 8056 E02611 reports + Nikkei 8056 + tomokutchi article on BIPROGY SI 大手 旧日本ユニシス 大日本印刷の持分会社 + Round 2 AI productivity doubling plan verification + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 8056 + Yahoo Finance 8056 + BIPROGY IR + Nikkei BIPROGY
What happened: BIPROGY was renamed from 'Nihon Unisys Ltd.' in April 2022 — a major systems integrator providing system development / operations, IT solutions, and cloud infrastructure for finance (SMBC, regional banks), retail / distribution, manufacturing, and public sectors. The renaming was intended to establish independence from US Unisys Corp. and to build a global brand. FY3/2026 revenue ¥433.69 billion (+7.3%), operating profit ¥42.60 billion (+9.1%), net profit ¥31.21 billion (+15.7%) — 4 consecutive years of record-high earnings. FY3/2027 guidance: revenue +8.4% (¥470 billion), operating profit +13.6% (¥48.4 billion), net profit +3.2% (¥32.2 billion) — 5th consecutive year of record-highs expected. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'sustained DX demand from financial institutions,' 'retail / distribution system-rebuild demand,' and 'growth in cloud-infrastructure / data-center services.' Mega bank (especially SMBC, a longstanding major customer) and regional bank core-system renewal, retail / distribution business-system rebuilds, and manufacturing smart-factory work continue. Services using the proprietary cloud / data-center base are growing as stock-type revenue, contributing to margin improvement. Stock reaction: BIPROGY stock rose over +50% in 2025. Structural drivers: First, 4 consecutive years of record-high earnings. Among SI companies, four straight years is a quality-growth hallmark — aligns with institutional Quality-Growth screens. The next-year guide also projects a 5th-year record, sustaining long-term growth expectations. Second, sustained large dividend hikes. Dividend ¥110 (prior) → ¥130 (current) → ¥140 (next-year plan). Higher payout ratio combined with absolute-amount growth from profit expansion attracted income-oriented institutional and retail investors. Third, long-term sustainability of financial-institution DX spending. IT investment by main customers (led by SMBC) is part of mega-bank-alliance long-term strategy, expected to sustain for the next 5-10 years. BIPROGY has established its position as a core IT vendor to the SMBC group. Fourth, operating margin improvement to 9.8%. Above SI-industry average (7-9%); structural reform and shift to high-value services are lifting margins. Fifth, brand refresh from renaming. The 'Nihon Unisys → BIPROGY' rename clarified the relationship with US Unisys — independent-company evaluation has risen. Barriers to global expansion and new-customer acquisition have also lowered. Risks: (a) financial-institution IT-spending reviews, (b) labor-cost inflation from IT-talent competition, (c) intensifying competition for large projects vs NTT Data, Fujitsu, TIS etc. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥433.686 billion (+7.3%), OP ¥42.604 billion (+9.1%), NP ¥31.209 billion (+15.7%) — growth in both. FY27 NP guide ¥32.2 billion (+3.2%) — 4 consecutive years of record-high earnings expected. Catalina Marketing Japan acquisition + sustained IT-investment demand drove earnings. AI / cloud area strengthening: Data & AI Innovation Lab uses industry-knowledge consultants + industry-specific business-scenario templates to embed AI into operations. Dividend upward-revised ¥120 → ¥130, FY27 plan ¥140 (+¥10) — stronger shareholder returns. 12 consecutive quarters of improvement; net/OP margin, revenue, EPS all uptrending; equity ratio sustained.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):
2025: stock flat/—; bucket=profitability_inflection.
Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (peak — price ahead of fundamentals):* the stock hit an all-time high of ¥6,594 on Aug 13 2025, with the business still growing only ~7%. The fundamentals were fine but the re-rating had outrun them — a ◎◎ where price is arguably ahead of the business. 業績 ◎ / 株価 ◎ → Q1, medium confidence (valuation stretched; this is the "watch for the flip" case, not a clean buy).
Inbound / demographic tailwind 3 names
In plain terms: companies riding a long-duration consumer / population shift in Japan. Examples in this bucket: istyle (@cosme captures inbound tourist spending on Japanese cosmetics), Sharing Technology (rides the aging-society demand for life-services — Japan aging rate going from 29.3% in 2024 to 38.7% by 2070), Smaregi (rides the inbound tourism + cashless-payment shift through cloud POS for restaurants and retail). Rule used: ticker-specific override for names whose Round 2-documented 2025 driver was a structural demographic / inbound theme. Why this matters: these tailwinds are durable on a decade scale — demographics and consumer-behavior shifts do not reverse quickly. But there is geopolitical / FX risk (inbound flows depend on yen weakness and travel policy stability), and competitive intensity can squeeze margins as more players chase the same growth. What to watch: monthly tourist-arrival stats (JNTO data), JPY exchange rate (yen weakness is the inbound multiplier), and demographic data points like single-household ratios and elderly-spending shares.
3660 istyle Inc. internet_platform small
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +6.1%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 3660 + istyle IR FY6/2026 Q1 + minkabu 3660 + kabutan 3660 + 株予報Pro 3660 + minkabu 3660 + nikkei 3660 + kabuyoho 3660 + istyle.co.jp Q1 settlement journal verification on growth + HK risk + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 3660 + minkabu 3660 + Yahoo Finance 3660
What happened: istyle is the comprehensive cosmetics-industry platform operator behind '@cosme' — Japan's largest cosmetics review / word-of-mouth site. Around the @cosme core, it operates @cosme STORE (physical stores nationwide), @cosme TOKYO (Harajuku flagship), @cosme SHOPPING (e-commerce), brand-side marketing support (Brand Official, sampling, review-data analytics for makers), and global expansion (Hong Kong, Taiwan, China, Southeast Asia). FY6/2026 (fiscal year ends in June) interim results: revenue ¥40.09 billion (+21.2%), operating profit ¥1.84 billion (+23.0%) — strong growth in Marketing Support and Retail. Full-year guide: revenue ¥83.0 billion (+20.7%), operating profit ¥3.8 billion (+20.1%). Why: Profit growth was driven by three pillars: 'sustained Marketing Support business growth (rising advertising / sampling / data-sales revenue from cosmetics brands),' 'Retail business store-sales expansion + inbound-tourist consumption capture,' and 'EC new-customer acquisition and successful sales events.' Global business is temporarily in loss due to Hong Kong flagship opening costs, but Asia brand expansion is positioned as a medium-long-term growth axis. Stock reaction: istyle stock rose over +50% in 2025. Structural drivers: First, emblematic inbound-recovery stock. Foreign-tourist purchases at physical stores (including @cosme TOKYO) surged — the market recognizes the strong cosmetics × inbound linkage. Japanese cosmetics-brand popularity among Korean / Chinese / Southeast Asian tourists is a structural tailwind. Second, high-margin Marketing Support business. Brand-side data sales using @cosme review / browsing data, ad placements, and sampling programs carry high gross margins — a leverage structure where profit grows faster than revenue. Third, platform power. Cosmetics makers cannot ignore @cosme's data and consumer reach; @cosme placement has become essential brand infrastructure. Rival platforms (LIPS etc.) are catching up, but istyle has the depth of maker relationships. Fourth, optionality of global expansion. Hong Kong, Taiwan, China, Southeast Asia expansion is loss-leading short-term, but capturing Asia cosmetics-market growth carries large long-term optionality. Fifth, two-year V-shape earnings recovery. After COVID-era stagnation, the inbound recovery + Marketing Support strengthening produced two consecutive years of revenue + profit growth. Risks: (a) geopolitical risk to inbound demand (Chinese travel restrictions, JPY appreciation reducing visits etc.), (b) prolonged losses in Global business, (c) advertiser-acquisition competition vs rival platforms. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY6/2026 H1: Revenue ¥40.089 billion (+21.2%), OP ¥1.839 billion (+23.0%). Q3: revenue ¥59.694 billion (+19.7%), OP ¥2.884 billion (+23.0%) — sharp growth. EC growth standout (platform partnerships + sales events). New stores + existing-store growth + traffic/repeat increases. High-marginal-revenue model means top-line growth flows to profitability. 12 consecutive quarters of improvement; net/OP margins recovering, equity ratio rising, EPS lifting. ROE/ROA above benchmarks. Note: Global (Hong Kong flagship) loss-widening on launch costs but post-opening already contributing.

3989 Sharingtechnology, Inc. internet_platform small
Revenue: n/a Stock 2025 YoY: n/a
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 3989 + Nikkei TDnet 2025/11/14 IFRS 短信 + kabutan news 2期ぶり最高益・15円増配 + 四季報 3989 + Nikkei TDnet 2025/5/15 Q2 + buffett-code 3989 + IFIS 株予報 3989 + Sharing Tech IR FY9/2025 短信 verification on 6,700 stores + 29.3%→38.7% aging rate + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 3989 + kabuyoho 3989 + Sharing Technology IR
What happened: Sharing Technology operates a matching platform for solving 'life's troubles' (plumbing issues, lost keys, pest control, junk removal, AC cleaning, end-of-life / funeral services, grave-closing services, etc.) — connecting service providers with consumers. Through its own sites ('Seikatsu 110-ban,' 'Kurashi no Convenience' etc.), the company networks small / medium service providers nationwide and instantly matches the right provider to consumer needs — an online intermediation business model. FY9/2025 (fiscal year ends in September) results (IFRS): revenue ¥8.58 billion (+14.4%), operating profit ¥2.07 billion (+15.9%) — growth in both. 5 consecutive years of revenue growth. Net profit ¥1.41 billion. FY9/2026 guidance: net profit ¥1.6 billion (+13.2%) — record-high earnings for the second consecutive time. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'management-resource focus on the life's-troubles business.' The company several years back divested peripheral businesses and concentrated resources on the core matching business. As a result: continued growth in monthly inquiries via the site, expansion of the provider network, improved AI-driven matching precision, and rising ARPU happened together — accelerating profit growth. Stock reaction: Sharing Technology stock rose over +30% in 2025. Structural drivers: First, 5-year sustained revenue-growth record. A continuous-growth streak aligns with institutional Quality-Growth strategy screens. Second, major dividend hike (¥40 → ¥55, +37.5%). The ¥15 increase signals management confidence in profit growth and was rewarded by the market. Higher payout ratio clarified shareholder-return policy for institutional investors. Third, structural tailwind from aging society. Lost keys, water leaks, pest control, funerals, grave-closing services — demand for 'life's troubles' services expands structurally with the rise in single-person and elderly households. Japan's demographics (aging, atomization) ensure this market expands medium-long term. Fourth, platform-business scale effects. The more matches occur, the more providers and consumers join — classic marketplace network effects that improve unit economics. Once scale is established, competitor entry becomes a high MOAT. Fifth, post-M&A integration synergies. Integration of previously acquired businesses is progressing — cross-sell and cost-reduction synergies are materializing. Risks: (a) intensifying competition with rival platforms (Kurashi no Market, Mitsumore, Your Meister etc.), (b) quality management of the provider network (brand damage if complaints arise), (c) rising consumer-marketing costs. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY9/2026 Q1: Revenue ¥2.276 billion (+17.2%), OP ¥0.533 billion (+9.5%) — growth in both. H1 NP ¥0.66 billion (+13.0%) — continued growth. In-house construction segment revenue grew significantly, ~6,400 affiliated stores partnered, Platform + In-house both engines firing. Platform business affiliate fees are the growth driver. Market cap ¥27.8 billion, PER 17.4x, ROE 27.4%, equity ratio 69.3% — high profitability + growth profile. Asset Value Investors Limited filed large-shareholder report — institutional investor interest rising. Abundant net cash + sustained growth expectations tracking with stock.
Pattern (bucket-templated label):

Source prose (fixture rawExplanation)
*2025 (record-year #2 — clean ◎):* stock ¥813 (Jan 10) – ¥1,262 (Aug 14); new multi-year high ¥1,262. **FY9/2025 actual (announced Nov 14, 2025): revenue ¥8,580M (+14.4% YoY), operating profit ¥2,074M (+15.9%)** — 2-year-highest profit, **2nd consecutive year of record results**, **annual dividend lifted ¥15** (capital return signal — alpha-menu U67 territory) per kabutan + IR disclosure. Sign summary: revenue rose +14.4% YoY; operating profit rose +15.9%; record results; profit margin expanding; dividend hike of ¥15; shares rallied to ¥1,262 multi-year high (Aug 14); business strong, stock strong. 業績 ◎ / 株価 ◎ (Q1 Compounder, third year of the rally).

Cautious side: stock has rallied ~15x off the Jan 2022 ¥84 low — valuation has run substantially; the underlying business is genuinely growing but at +14-20% revenue, not at the rate the stock has appreciated; multi-year compounder story is real but stock multiple is now elevated; market for "home-trouble platform" services is steady but not infinite — TAM cap matters.

4431 Smaregi, Inc. SaaS small
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +14.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 4431 + Smaregi IR financial-results + minkabu 4431 + 四季報 4431 + Yahoo news fisco 26/4期3Q + buffett-code 4431 + kabutore 4431 chart + moneyworld 4431 + buffett-code 4431 + asset-alive 4431 verification on +68.5% cashless growth + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: Smaregi IR + minkabu 4431 + Nikkei 4431
What happened: Smaregi is a SaaS company specializing in cloud POS (point-of-sale) systems for retail / restaurants — iPad-based intuitive POS, sales analytics, inventory management, cashless payment integration ('PAYGATE'), labor management ('Smaregi Time Card'), all delivered via subscription. Main customers are SMB restaurants, retailers, and service businesses; Smaregi holds a leading share in Japan's cloud-POS market. FY4/2026 (May 2025 - Apr 2026) Q3 YTD revenue ¥9.62 billion (+22.1%), operating profit ¥2.23 billion (+22.8%), with ARR (annual recurring revenue) crossing ¥10 billion — ahead of the 2nd mid-term plan's FY2026 target (¥9.46 billion). Full-year guide: revenue ¥13.15 billion (+18.8%), operating profit ¥2.95 billion (+24.1%). Why: Profit growth was driven by 'cross-selling (additional services) around POS' and 'hardware-subscription plan refresh.' Selling additional services (time card, payment, retail analytics, AI demand forecasting etc.) to existing customers on a monthly add-on basis is succeeding — ARPU continues to rise. Hardware-subscription refresh also improved equipment revenue. Stock reaction: Smaregi stock rose over +50% in 2025. Structural drivers: First, ARR crossing ¥10 billion. For SaaS names, crossing the ¥10 billion ARR mark is treated as a 'mid-cap → large-cap SaaS step-up' signal by institutional investors — early achievement was significantly rewarded. Second, ripple effects from the invoice system and revised e-storage law. Small-business digitalization demand continues; replacement of paper registers / legacy POS with cloud POS continues. Third, cross-sell-driven ARPU expansion model. Standalone POS faces commoditization risk, but bundling payment / labor / analytics raises per-customer revenue and lowers churn — the 'platform strategy' is paying off. Fourth, dividend initiation and continued growth. Smaregi initiated dividends earlier than typical SaaS names, attracting long-term and income-oriented investors. FY26 year-end dividend guidance ¥20 (prior ¥15). Fifth, inbound demand + cashless tailwind. Foreign-visitor growth is driving restaurant / retail demand recovery and new store openings. New-store cloud-POS demand is a structural tailwind. Risks: (a) price competition with POS+ (POS Plus), Square, Ubiregi, etc., (b) SMB cyclical sensitivity, (c) payment-related risks (chargebacks etc.). [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] FY4/2026 H1: Revenue ¥6.264 billion, OP ¥1.321 billion — solid, ARR ¥9.94 billion achieved (mid-term plan ahead of schedule). FY4/2025 full: revenue ¥11.066 billion, OP ¥2.375 billion, ordinary ¥2.358 billion. FY4/2026 guide: revenue ¥13.859 billion (+25.2%), OP ¥2.804 billion (+18.1%). +48.6% YoY growth rate, ARR ¥8.19 billion. POS-core cross-sell + equipment subscription drove upward revision. Churn 0.5%, ARR share ~90% of revenue — robust business base. Equity ratio 69.2%, net assets ¥8.914 billion. Vertical SaaS market: $135B in 2025 → $194B by 2029 (Japan retail/restaurant niche benefits). PER 22.4x forecast. METI promoting smart-register adoption — policy tailwind.
