Sector-specific tailwind — content/IP/defense category re-rating 3 names
In plain terms: companies riding a category-level tailwind that the market is actively re-pricing. Examples in this bucket: Toho (rode a single mega-hit film 国宝 — 2025's No.1 box-office with ¥5.6 billion gross in 38 days), TV Tokyo (rode the global re-rating of anime IP via overseas streaming — Crunchyroll, Netflix, Prime Video license fees rising), SKY Perfect JSAT (rode Japan's defense-spending ramp — Defense Ministry satellite constellation contract + SpaceX launch contracts doubling capacity). Rule used: ticker-specific override for names whose Round 2-documented 2025 driver was a specific sector / category tailwind. Why this matters: these tailwinds are typically multi-year — the defense budget is targeted at 2% of GDP through FY27, the anime overseas market keeps growing, and mega-hit films validate IP-driven re-rating. But the company needs to be BEST-IN-CLASS in its niche to keep capturing the tailwind; weaker players in the same category may underperform. What to watch: budget cycles (defense procurement schedules), streaming-platform license-fee trends (anime), and the next IP / property catalyst (film slate, sequel announcements, new IP acquisitions).
9412 SKY Perfect JSAT Corporation telecom mid
Revenue: unknown Stock 2025 YoY: +119.4%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 9412 + Nikkei TDnet 2026/4/27 短信 + logmi finance article on record earnings + LIMO article on 3-month stock move + irbank E04497 reports + Nikkei Veritas article on SKY Perfect JSAT space-defense 2x stock-price + SpaceX launch contract verification + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: kabutan 9412 + kabuyoho 9412 + Nikkei 9412
What happened: SKY Perfect JSAT Holdings is Asia's largest satellite operator, with two pillars: 'Space business' (satellite communications/broadcasting, satellite imaging, defense-related space intelligence) and 'Media business' (SKY PerfecTV! pay-TV, Wowow, etc.). FY3/2026 revenue ¥127.58 billion (+3.1%), operating profit ¥35.27 billion (+28.3%) — a record-high OP. Net profit ¥23.31 billion (+22.0%). Beat the revised guidance. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'Space business growth' and 'Media business cost-cutting effects.' Space revenue ¥69.8 billion (+7.9%), segment profit ¥16.1 billion (+5.6%) — domestic satellite-comm demand, plus the Ministry of Defense satellite constellation order (constellation = many small satellites operating in coordination) and growth in Space Intelligence (satellite imagery analysis). Media revenue ¥64.3 billion (-1.9%) — slightly down, but segment profit ¥7.7 billion (+74.3%) — distribution competition is squeezing revenue, but fixed-cost compression and efficiency drove margins sharply higher. Stock reaction: Stock rose +30.7% from ¥2,873 (3-Mar) to ¥3,755 (2-Jun-2026). On annual basis the rise is larger (over +50%). Structural drivers behind the stock-up: First, re-rating on defense / national-security themes. With the Japanese government targeting defense spending of 2% of GDP (by FY27), defense-related stocks broadly re-rated; SKY Perfect JSAT — a rare private-sector company with a concrete government-related Defense Ministry satellite-constellation contract — drew attention. Worsening security environment is a long-term tailwind. Second, record-high earnings + beating guidance. Beating the revised guidance, 3 consecutive years of OP growth, and record OP / net profit simultaneously — a 'clean' good-earnings story that institutional investors easily digest. Third, strong FY27 guidance. FY3/2027 guidance: revenue +5.8% (¥135 billion), operating profit +10.6% (¥39 billion), net profit +15.8% (¥27 billion) — sustained double-digit profit growth outlook. Fourth, dividend policy and shareholder returns. Dividend yield basis appreciated by income-oriented institutional investors. Fifth, high-growth Space Intelligence business. Satellite imagery analytics, IoT data linkage and other new areas are viewed by the market as 'Japan's Maxar (the US satellite-imagery company)' with growth optionality. Risks: (a) delays in defense-related orders or government-budget volatility, (b) Media business structural decline outpacing cost cuts, (c) satellite-launch failures or other operational risks. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥127.6 billion (+3.1%), OP ¥35.3 billion (+28.3%), ordinary ¥35.4 billion (+29.8%) — sharp growth. FY27 ordinary profit guide ¥39 billion (+10.1%) — 4th consecutive year of record-high earnings expected. Space business is the driver — transformation from telecom into defense play; satellite imagery as 'eye for counter-attack capability.' 12 consecutive quarters of margin improvement, equity ratio rising + debt declining, revenue+EPS expanding.

FY3/2025 OP ¥27.5B (+3.6%), NP ¥19.1B (+8.0%). **NAMED DESCRIPTIVE STOCK-MOVE DRIVER (verified at source)**: Japan's expanded defense and space budget drove the rerating of Sky Perfect JSAT's satellite-communications business — the satellite business is positioned for material government-budget capture as Japan's defense spending in space programs scales. **Context (NOT counted as evidence per describe-not-predict taxonomy)**: Goldman Sachs initiated coverage with a Buy rating in 2025 (broker recommendation — predictive, kept as color only); independent analyst projections that space business could contribute ~90% of Sky Perfect JSAT's operating profit by FY3/2029 (forward projection — predictive, kept as color only). 業績 ◎ (OP/NP up) / 株価 ◎ (close +119%, more than doubled on the defense-budget-driven rerating).

9413 TV TOKYO Holdings Corporation broadcaster mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +41.8%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Yahoo Finance 9413 + Nikkei TDnet 2026/5/8 短信 + 松井証券 9413 + minkabu 9413 + analyst commentary on NARUTO/孤独のグルメ + minkabu 9413 + finboard 9413 + kabutan 9413 + Nikkei 9413 verification on 68.9% equity ratio + 4-pillar strategy + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: minkabu 9413 + Nikkei 9413 + TV Tokyo IR mid-term plan
What happened: TV Tokyo Holdings is a content-oriented major media group centered on terrestrial broadcasting (TV Tokyo), with anime / distribution business (including overseas), content sales, BS TV Tokyo (satellite), publishing, and licensing. FY3/2026 revenue ¥164.92 billion (+5.8%), operating profit ¥11.40 billion (+46.4%) — a record-high OP. Why: Profit growth was driven by 'overseas-revenue expansion in anime/streaming business.' Unlike other key broadcasters, TV Tokyo's business model is built on anime and content sales rather than news/variety ('Naruto,' 'Boruto,' 'Pokémon,' 'Demon Slayer' (co-production), 'Spy x Family,' 'Solitary Gourmet' etc.) — overseas distribution license sales and IP licensing drove profit. Anime demand in North America and East Asia is rising, with double-digit growth in distribution-rights fees from global platforms like Crunchyroll, Netflix, Prime Video. Stock reaction: TV Tokyo HD stock rose +41.8% in 2025, driven by 'profit expansion as a content company' and 're-rating from undervalued levels' together. Structural stock-up drivers: First, long-term cash-flow value of anime IP. Long-lived IPs like 'Naruto' and 'Pokémon' generate royalties over decade-plus horizons — stock-type revenue. The market is shifting evaluation from 'broadcaster' toward 'content-rights holder' (a category where higher PERs are accepted). Second, rise of overseas streaming platforms. Distribution-rights fees paid by Crunchyroll, Netflix, Prime Video, Disney+ for anime content are rising year-over-year — TV Tokyo is in a 'seller's market.' Rising overseas-revenue ratio also hedges FX risk. Third, dividend-hike policy and PBR improvement. TV Tokyo HD is also responding to the TSE PBR-improvement request — lifting payout ratio and conducting buybacks. Yield improvement attracted both retail and institutional buying. Fourth, differentiated business mix vs other key broadcasters. NTV / TBS / TV Asahi / Fuji are highly dependent on terrestrial ad revenue, while TV Tokyo has a higher content/licensing-revenue ratio — making earnings less swingy with ad-market cycles. This earned a 'stable-growth' premium. Fifth, 'Solitary Gourmet' global expansion. Live-action drama is also gaining international popularity, broadening the content portfolio. Risks: (a) drought in new anime IP hits, (b) downward pressure on platform license fees (platform-side bargaining power rising), (c) production-cost inflation (voice actor / studio rate increases) compressing margins. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥164.92 billion (+5.8%), OP ¥11.40 billion (+46.4%), NP ¥7.7 billion (+27.61%) — record. Equity ratio 68.9%. Growth engine 'Anime/Distribution' — NARUTO/BORUTO overseas game royalty income drove segment profit 1.5x. Broadcasting CPM raises also helped. Mid-term plan: CaaS (Content-as-a-Service) strategy + global IP-media transformation. FY28 guidance: revenue ¥165 billion, OP ¥11.5 billion. PER 15.7x forecast, PBR 1.12x, yield 2.21%.

9602 TOHO CO.,LTD content_games mid
Revenue: up Stock 2025 YoY: +29.6%
Genuine research (JP web search per company) 📚 Toho IR 決算説明資料 2026/2 + kabutan 9602 + minkabu 9602 + 株予報Pro 9602 + Yahoo News article on 『国宝』 hit + 10.9% stock rise despite profit decline + ticket price hike + single-pass combined-prompt verification 2026-06: gamebiz 9602 + kabutan 9602 + Toho IR + Nikkei 9602
What happened: Toho is Japan's largest film production, distribution, and exhibition company, known for distributing major hits like 'Godzilla,' 'Shin Godzilla,' 'Your Name,' and the Demon Slayer movies. Beyond film, they run real estate (including the TOHO Cinemas chain and building rentals), theater (TOHO Musicals), and an anime business. FY2/2026 results were strong — operating revenue ¥360.6 billion (+15.2%), operating profit ¥67.9 billion (+5.0%) — both up. Why: The biggest contribution came from two megahits: the latest Demon Slayer film and 'Kokuhō.' The film business was strong, and the IP/Anime business also grew. The structure works like this: when one of Toho's accumulated hit-chain characters/IPs lands a megahit, related merchandise, licensing revenue, and streaming-rights revenue all rise alongside. Stock reaction: This is a textbook case where fundamentals improvement and stock price move in lockstep upward. Several structural reasons explain why business and stock are rising together. First, fortress balance sheet. Toho's equity ratio is well above the 30% considered healthy, and interest-bearing debt is trending down. Insolvency risk is essentially zero, making it an easy-to-hold defensive name for institutional investors. Second, profitability improvement. Net margin has improved YoY across many quarters, and ROE/ROA meet conventional targets — profitability is stable. Quarter-to-quarter the film-hit calendar creates volatility, but the long-term trend is up. Third, stable real-estate revenue base. In addition to running cinemas, Toho owns substantial real estate in the Tokyo metropolitan area (the TOHO building, etc.), with rental income providing a stable base. This supports profit even in film-business weak periods. Fourth, adaptation to the streaming era. Licensing to Netflix and Amazon Prime Video, overseas distribution, and merchandise expansion — Toho is expanding revenue streams to fit the digital age. With Demon Slayer as the model, the 'film → anime → merchandise → live event' IP-extension monetization is now established. Fifth, Toho brand reliability. As the established film-industry incumbent, the Toho brand provides negotiation power in the content industry — getting source-IP rights, securing streaming deals, etc. That's a powerful intangible asset. This is an orthodox case of fundamentals driving stock — heavy institutional long-term holding demand. [Single-pass combined-prompt verification (2026-06)] Revenue ¥360.66 billion (+15.2%), OP ¥67.89 billion (+5.0%), ordinary ¥70.14 billion (+8.8%), NP ¥51.77 billion (+19.4%) — record. Film segment: revenue ¥182.6 billion (+30.6%), OP ¥37.3 billion (+30.3%). Cinema admissions ¥49M (+27.6%), distribution revenue ¥139.9 billion (historic record). Major hits: Demon Slayer Infinity Castle Chapter 1 + Kokuhō (2025 No.1, ¥5.6B box office) + Detective Conan + Chainsaw Man. Strategic moves: Warner Bros. JPN distribution deal (from 2026), ¥5 billion TOHO-ONE customer data base, ¥13 billion buyback (7.5M shares). Analyst target ¥1,864 (4 strong-buy, 1 buy, 4 neutral). IP/anime profit target +200% by 2032 vs FY25.
